          🏢All Clients
          Applied Materials

          AMAT Austin Program — Overview

          End-to-End Workflow Sequence — Pipeline Build → Req Fulfillment → Engagement. Austin, TX & Future CA Sites.

          📋
          Total Steps
          53
          ▶
          Phases
          9
          💻
          Systems
          7
          👥
          Owner Roles
          7
          🔍What This Program Is

          This document maps every action in the AMAT bulk hiring workflow to a specific system, owner, and set of artifacts. It covers the full pipeline from initial sourcing through active engagement at Austin, TX (future CA sites TBD). V2 — Updated April 20, 2026.

          Two-Lane Onboarding Model — Dual Approach:

          Pre-Onboarding (Phase 4)

          Captures assessments, photo, banking, BGC, drug screening, and Raise employment documents. CS works remotely to drive portal completion; On-Site Manager acts as the physical gate.

          In-Onboarding (Phase 7)

          Triggered after AMAT selection. Covers I-9 verification, required H&S training, AMAT agreements, and badge activation. Same dual approach — CS remote + On-Site gate.

          Key handoff: Curation → Contractor Success at Step 35 (work order acceptance). The Contractor Portal is the source of truth for all worker-entered data.

          ▶Phase Map — V2
          PH 1 Sourcing — Opptly → Salesforce — Steps 1–3
          PH 2 Screening — Salesforce / Avature — Steps 4–7
          PH 3 COI, Rehire & Conditional Offer — Salesforce → AMAT Review → Portal Invite — Steps 8–13
          PH 4 On-Site Assessment & Pre-Onboarding — Non-linear: CS remote + On-Site gate — Steps 14–20
          PH 5 Talent Pool — Fieldglass Workforce Module — Steps 21–24
          PH 6 Req Fulfillment — AMAT Selection — Fieldglass + Salesforce — Steps 25–34
          PH 7 In-Onboarding & Activation — 'Tuesday Badge Day' — Steps 35–45 — ⇨ CS Handoff at Step 35
          PH 8 Training & Shift Start — On-Site + Fieldglass — Steps 46–47
          PH 9 Engagement & Ongoing — Fieldglass + Contractor App (TX only at launch) — Steps 48–53
          ✎My Notes
          Applied Materials

          Systems & Owner Legend

          All systems and owner roles referenced in the AMAT workflow. V2 — Updated April 20, 2026.

          💻Systems
          SystemDescription
          OpptlyOpptly Connect: Job postings, applications, talent community registration. Opptly Curate: Matches candidates to jobs.
          SalesforceRaise CRM — candidate, job, and job submission management; candidate engagement and talent pooling.
          Fieldglass (VMS)SAP Fieldglass — AMAT's vendor management system for workforce module, submissions, work orders, timesheets, and invoicing (AMAT self-invoices).
          Contractor AppRaise proprietary portal — source of truth for worker-entered data. Worker profiles, banking, tax forms, employment docs, H&S training, onboarding. Salesforce initiates portal activation; two-step process (Step 1 at pre-onboarding, Step 2 at placement).
          AMAT (ServiceNow)AMAT's system for BGC/drug screening initiation and COI/rehire submission. BGC conducted through AMAT — AMAT pays for all screenings.
          NMORaise time entry plugin (connected to Contractor App) — CA meal/rest break compliance. Not relevant for TX launch. Will revisit when CA sites come online.
          On-Site / PhysicalIn-person activities at AMAT Austin site — assessments, drug screens, badge pickup, training.
          👥Owner Roles
          OwnerScope
          RecruiterRaise recruitment team — sources and screens candidates from job boards and public candidate databases via Avature.
          CurationCuration team — manages the private AMAT talent community in Salesforce; advertises branded jobs; matches talent community members to jobs; pools candidates; handles client communication and COI/submission workflow. Fully dedicated to the account.
          On-Site ManagerRaise on-site presence — manages the assigned workforce as the first point of contact for workers. Walks facilities, works with client VMO to understand upcoming demand. Manages assessments, conditional offers, drug/BGC initiation during pre-onboarding. Acts as the physical gate for pre-onboarding and in-onboarding completion. Functions as Account Manager with CS responsibilities.
          Contractor Success (CS)Raise CS team — validates placement setup at Step 29; takes over fully at work order acceptance (Step 35); owns in-onboarding, ongoing engagement, and timesheet validation. Works remotely to drive portal completion before and during on-site visits.
          AMAT VMO / Hiring LeaderApplied Materials — reviews candidates, selects candidates for specific job orders, manages reqs, activates resources, approves time.
          Worker / CandidateThe individual moving through the pipeline.
          System / AutoAutomated system actions — API syncs, status updates, PO generation.
          ✎My Notes
          Applied Materials — Full Onboarding Workflow

          Candidate Onboarding & Employment Workflow

          9 Phases • 60 Steps • Candidate journey + all internal steps, from sourcing to active employment • Austin, TX. Merges EOR Onboarding Workflow, End-to-End Sequence, and SF Portal Workflow.

          🚨 Critical Deadlines — Wednesday Training Start

          Thu/FriAll supplier activities complete (Friday 10AM CST absolute latest) MondayPhoto submitted to AMAT — white background only. Late = 2–3 hr wait on badge day. TuesdayBadge pickup on-site. Worker presents valid ID. Raise rep present. Each supplier gets a time slot. WednesdayClassroom training begins 8AM

          👷 Roles in Scope (Austin, TX)

          Manufacturing Technicians • Engineering Technicians • Material Handlers • Material Coordinators

          Order-to-Start Timeline

          ~5–7 days from resource order to activation • 8-day paid training
          Raise / Curation
          Contractor Success (CS)
          Delivery
          Automated trigger
          AMAT action
          Milestone
          ◆ Gate / Open item
          Phase 1 — Sourcing
          Opptly → Salesforce — Steps 1–3
          Steps 1–3
          Sourcing and Screening work in parallel. RaiseONE Recruiters work in Avature from public channels. Curation team works in the private AMAT Salesforce community. Screened candidates from Avature auto-import to Salesforce when ready. Non-traditional sourcing includes job fairs, community colleges, and student outreach.
          #ActionSystemOwnerArtifacts / Notes
          1Post job to Opptly and SalesforceOpptly / SFCurationJob description; pay rate; shift info
          2Source candidates — branded ads, social media, referrals, job fairs, collegesSF & AvatureCuration / RecruiterCandidate profiles
          First-in-process dynamic: candidates already with another AMAT supplier are excluded. On-Site Manager may source at community events. Leverage existing Opptly talent pools.
          3Candidate data syncs to Salesforce via API; SF record createdSalesforceSystem (API)SF Candidate Record
          Phase 2 — Screening
          Salesforce / Avature — Steps 4–7
          Steps 4–7
          #ActionSystemOwnerArtifacts / Notes
          4Administer screening questionsSF / AvatureCuration / RecruiterScreening responses
          Do NOT ask about criminal history directly. Cover: (1) BGC willingness — role requires BGC through AMAT, comfortable proceeding? (2) Physical demands — 45–55 hrs/wk, stand all day in cleanroom (3) Supplier pool check — already with another AMAT supplier? (4) Shift/schedule — rotating shifts, 8-day training (5) Steel-toe safety shoes required. Disqualifiers surfaced via First Advantage BGC only. Three-tier system: Eligible / Review / Ineligible.
          5Schedule and conduct phone screenSF / AvatureCuration / RecruiterPhone screen notes
          Confirm: shift availability, steel-toe shoes, transportation to Austin site, willingness for 8-day paid training (8AM–5PM), ability to work 45–60 hrs/week and stand all day in cleanroom.
          6Mark candidate ‘Ready for Pre-Submission’SalesforceCuration / Recruiter
          Two distinct submissions in this workflow: (1) Pre-Submission here → advances candidate to on-site assessment, BGC, drug screen, then Fieldglass Workforce Module. (2) Formal Submission in Phase 6 → when AMAT VMO instructs Raise to attach a worker to a specific active requisition.
          Phase 3 — COI, Rehire Check & Conditional Offer
          Salesforce → AMAT VMO Review → Contractor Portal Invite — Steps 7–12
          Steps 7–12
          #ActionSystemOwnerArtifacts / Notes
          7Issue COI (Conflict of Interest) form to candidate via SalesforceSalesforceCurationCOI form issued
          COI attaches to contact record in SF. Covers: personal relationships with AMAT employees, outside business activities, board positions, financial interests in AMAT suppliers/customers/competitors.
          8Candidate completes and signs COI; submit to AMAT VMOSalesforceWorker / CurationCOI form (signed)
          9If former AMAT worker: complete Rehire form; submit both COI + Rehire to AMAT VMOSalesforceCurationRehire form (signed)
          10◆ GATE — Former AMAT workers only: hold for VMO rehire eligibility review (up to 5 business days). Do NOT proceed until approved.AMATAMAT VMORehire eligibility: Eligible / Review / Ineligible. Rejected = flagged in SF, do not delete record.
          11Issue conditional offer letter; trigger Contractor Portal invite (Portal Step 1 opens)SF / Contractor AppCurationConditional offer letter; portal invite; welcome email
          Conditional offer issued before on-site assessment. Conditional on: passing skills assessment, clearing BGC + drug screen, and AMAT selecting the worker for an assignment. Portal invite includes credential request for badge-ready photo (shoulders up, plain background, no hat/glasses). Candidate can begin Portal Step 1 remotely before on-site visit.
          12Delivery edits candidate placement details in SF: assignee type, WC code, pay rate, work location, start dateSalesforceDeliveryUpdated SF placement record
          SF Portal Workflow — Handoff Step: This is where the resume arrives in SF and we move to pre-onboarding. Delivery owns editing all placement fields before the portal is auto-initiated.
          Phase 4 — On-Site Assessment & Pre-Onboarding
          On-Site + Contractor App — Steps 13–21 — Non-linear: CS remote + On-Site gate
          Steps 13–21
          Non-Linear Process: CS works remotely to encourage portal task completion (profile, banking, tax forms, Raise employment docs) in advance of and alongside the on-site visit. The On-Site Manager is the physical gate — no candidate progresses to the talent pool without all items verified. Goal: maximum completion by the time the candidate leaves the assessment site.
          #ActionSystemOwnerArtifacts / Notes
          13⚡ Automated — SF portal initiated: COI, profile creation, conditional offer, photo upload triggered. Skills assessment invite + Contractor App invite sent to candidate.Salesforce (Auto)SystemPortal invite; assessment invite; app invite
          14Candidate completes Portal Step 1 remotely: profile, banking (Plaid), tax forms (W-4, state)Contractor AppWorker / CS (remote)Name/address; banking info; tax forms; Portal Step 1 complete
          If not completed remotely, candidate completes on laptop on-site. CS follows up remotely to close gaps. Banking collected here for payroll setup. Portal is source of truth — worker-entered data creates clean audit trail.
          15Conduct on-site skills assessment (LEGO test for MT; Schematics + Multimeter for ET) and capture badge-ready photoOn-SiteOn-Site ManagerAssessment results (percentage score); badge-ready photo
          Capture percentage score, not just pass/fail — multiple skill levels exist; score required when loading profile into Workforce Module. Photo: shoulders up, plain background, no hat, no glasses. Saved as credential in Salesforce. NOT uploaded to Fieldglass until worker is assigned to a specific req. AMAT has a ‘Review’ tier — results are not always binary.
          16Assessment passed? If no → flag in Salesforce (retain record, do not delete). If yes → proceed.SalesforceOn-Site ManagerAssessment status logged
          17Capture Raise employment documents on-siteContractor AppOn-Site ManagerRaise employment agreement; Raise policies
          Raise-side documents captured while candidate is on-site. AMAT-side agreements wait until in-onboarding (Phase 7) after selection for an active req.
          18Initiate BGC (via AMAT ServiceNow → First Advantage) and drug screening (oral swab on-site)AMAT / On-SiteCurationBGC consent; drug screen consent; BGC initiated; drug test collected
          BGC: First Advantage, 7-year lookback, AMAT pays. Valid 6 months. Drug screen: oral swab on-site (transitioning to lab-based). Valid 30 days. If drug screen expires before placement, must retest immediately. Both consent forms collected at this step.
          ⚠ Confirm AMAT’s authorization form can prescribe both AMAT and Raise as requesting parties. Follow up with First Advantage on disclosure setup.
          19BGC and drug screen both cleared?AMATAMATBGC results; drug screen results
          If either fails → flag in Salesforce, do not delete record. Determine notification process: how/who is notified and what steps are taken. AMAT Security provides email update on BGC results.
          20Candidate completes COI form in Contractor PortalContractor AppWorkerCOI completion
          21On-Site Manager gate check — confirm all pre-onboarding items complete before talent pool entrySalesforceOn-Site ManagerPre-onboarding checklist complete
          Gate items: assessment passed (with score), photo on file, portal profile complete, Raise employment docs signed, COI complete, BGC initiated and cleared, drug screen cleared. Any gaps flagged to CS for remote follow-up before candidate can progress.
          Phase 5 — Talent Pool — Fieldglass Workforce Module
          Fieldglass + Salesforce — Steps 22–25 — Holding until AMAT assigns to active req
          Steps 22–25
          #ActionSystemOwnerArtifacts / Notes
          22Add candidate to Fieldglass Workforce ModuleFieldglassCurationFG workforce profile
          Not tied to a specific req. Photo and required documents uploaded only when worker is assigned to a specific req during formal submission (Phase 6). Multiple suppliers build talent pools in parallel — competitive dynamic.
          23Candidate status set to ‘Talent Pooled’ — holding until AMAT assigns to active reqFieldglassSystem
          24Weekly check-in on candidate availability; monitor BGC (6-month) and drug screen (30-day) expirySalesforceCurationAvailability log
          All updates completed by Friday so VMO has current data on Mondays. Automated Salesforce notifications when screening approaching expiry. Regular availability outreach (Sense or similar). If drug screen expires before placement, candidate must retest immediately or be removed from module.
          25Make H&S digital training available in Contractor App (completion NOT required until Phase 7 — but early completion reduces Tuesday activation load)Contractor AppCSH&S training modules available
          Phase 6 — Req Fulfillment, AMAT Selection & Offer
          Fieldglass + Salesforce — Steps 26–37 — SF Portal Workflow Phase 2 starts here
          Steps 26–37
          SF Portal Workflow — Phase 2 (Active Job Placement): Delivery moves candidate to OFFER in Fieldglass and updates the placement record. CS then changes the Salesforce workflow to Onboarding Workflow 2, which fires the second-half onboarding sequence — I-9, banking, AMAT docs, Raise docs, H&S training.
          #ActionSystemOwnerArtifacts / Notes
          26Hiring leader identifies hiring need; AMAT VMO creates req and approvesAMATHiring Leader / AMAT VMOReq / work order
          27AMAT releases req to supplier(s); Fieldglass distributes req to RaiseFieldglassAMAT VMO / System
          28Curation contacts available candidates from talent pool — confirm availability, shift, and start dateSalesforceCurationAvailability confirmation; start date
          Confirm drug screen within 30-day validity. If expired, candidate must retest before proceeding. Automated outreach via SF confirms candidate availability and interest in the specific shift and start date.
          29CS validates placement setup in Salesforce; unlocks Portal Step 2 for workerSalesforceCSSF job record; Portal Step 2 activated; contracting details confirmed
          Unlocking Portal Step 2 triggers in-onboarding items for worker (Rehire form, AMAT agreements). CS validates all placement details for data precision before activation. No extra approval layers for repetitive roles within budget.
          30Curation submits candidate in Fieldglass (Formal Submission) — profiles submitted for AMAT reviewFieldglassCurationFG candidate submission
          31AMAT reviews candidates in Fieldglass — candidates moved to Offer stageFieldglassAMAT VMOResource order created
          32Work order created; PO generated (4–6 hrs processing)FieldglassSystemWork order; purchase order
          33Raise accepts resource order in Fieldglass. Onboarding can begin while PO integration processes.FieldglassRaiseResource order accepted
          34Delivery: update offer placement — Job ID → current job, Submission ID → new submission, placement status → SubmittedSF / FieldglassDeliveryUpdated placement; submission ID
          35⚡ Automated — Phase 2 onboarding initiated: Submitted status fires onboarding sequence in SFSalesforce (Auto)System
          36CS changes Salesforce workflow to Onboarding Workflow 2 (I-9, banking, AMAT docs, Raise docs, H&S training)SalesforceCSOnboarding Workflow 2 active
          37CS sends worker start notification; prompts portal completion of in-onboarding itemsContractor AppCSStart notification; portal reminder
          Phase 7 — In-Onboarding & Activation — ‘Tuesday Badge Day’
          Contractor App + Fieldglass + On-Site — Steps 38–50 — EOR Stages 8–9
          Steps 38–50
          ⟶ Handoff to Contractor Success at Step 38. CS accepts the work order and owns the worker relationship from this point forward. CS reaches out pre-Tuesday to drive portal completion. On-Site Manager is the final gate — no worker proceeds to badge pickup without all items verified. Target: ~2-hour block on Tuesday — badge setup + final docs + Fieldglass confirmation.
          #ActionSystemOwnerArtifacts / Notes
          38⟶ HANDOFF — CS accepts work order. CS owns worker relationship from here.FieldglassCS
          39CS contacts worker pre-Tuesday — prompts I-9 Section 1, AMAT agreements, H&S training via portalContractor AppCSOutreach email; portal reminder
          40Worker completes I-9 Section 1 in portal; CS completes Section 2 (physical document review) on-site TuesdayContractor App / On-SiteWorker / CSI-9 form; identity/work authorization docs
          Section 1 completed by worker in portal remotely. Section 2: worker must present original identity and work authorization documents in person on Tuesday. I-9 must be completed within 3 business days of start date.
          41Complete Rehire form with worker (formal version — distinct from Phase 3 rehire eligibility check)Contractor AppCSRehire form (signed)
          ⚠ Clarify whether this is an AMAT or Raise form. Note: ‘rehires’ to AMAT may be first-time Raise workers.
          42Execute Contingent Worker Confidentiality Agreement (AMNA) and all AMAT-side artifacts with workerContractor AppCSAMNA; Confidentiality Agreement; Export Compliance Cert (Exhibit A)
          AMAT artifacts signed only after selection for active req — not during pre-onboarding. AMNA covers: confidentiality, IP, code of conduct, export compliance, arbitration clause. 18-month tenure limit. Executed copy uploaded to Fieldglass. Applied Travel Rules (Exhibit B): not required for EOR workers.
          ⚠ Confirm whether Export Compliance Cert (Exhibit A) is required for all bulk hire roles or only specific roles. Flag for confirmation with AMAT before go-live.
          43Confirm BGC clearance; Raise marks BGC complete in Fieldglass checklistFieldglassRaiseBGC clearance confirmed; Fieldglass checklist updated
          BGC already completed during pipeline build (Phase 4). AMAT Security provides email update on results. Raise marks complete in Fieldglass.
          44Worker completes H&S digital training (REQUIRED — enforced here if not done during pool stage)Contractor AppWorkerH&S training completion
          Confirm worker has steel-toe safety shoes. CS verifies completion if already done in Phase 5.
          45Worker completes Fieldglass onboarding tasksFieldglassCSFG onboarding tasks complete
          46On-Site Manager final gate check — all in-onboarding items verified before badge activationSalesforceOn-Site ManagerIn-onboarding checklist complete
          Gate items: I-9 complete, Rehire form signed, AMNA + AMAT agreements signed, H&S done, Fieldglass tasks complete. No worker proceeds to badge pickup without all items verified on-site.
          47Submit worker photo for badging (due Monday before training week — white background)AMATRaiseBadge-ready photo submitted
          🚨 Late photos = 2–3 hour wait on badge day while security processes on-site. Activation deadline: Thursday preferred, Friday 10AM CST absolute latest for Wednesday training start.
          48AMAT Security clears BGC and reviews COI; AMAT EW Ops activates resource order — Workday integration runs, Contractor ID generatedAMATAMAT VMO / SecurityCompliance clearance; Workday record; Contractor ID
          Workday integration runs at 3AM and 3PM CST. By Monday, Contractor IDs integrated into IT and badging systems. Badges pre-printed from Monday photo submission.
          49✅ TUESDAY — Worker reports on-site, receives badge, completes system access setup with AMAT IT. Raise rep present. Each supplier gets a time slot.On-SiteCS / On-Site MgrBadge issued; site access granted
          ⚠ Open: What if a worker misses Tuesday badge pickup? Backup slot process? How and when are time slots assigned to each supplier?
          50Fieldglass status updated to Active. Onboarding marked complete. Curation job updated to Assigned to Active Assignment.Fieldglass / SFCSFG status: Active
          Phase 8 — Training & Shift Start
          On-Site + Fieldglass — Steps 51–53 — EOR Stage 10
          Steps 51–53
          #ActionSystemOwnerArtifacts / Notes
          51Worker begins 8-day paid training — Wednesday 8AM. Day shift trains days; swing & night train during swing schedule.On-SiteAMAT (delivery) + Raise (support)Training attendance
          Training schedule — Wed–Fri Week 1: Classroom (foundational knowledge, safety, systems: evacuation, PPE, restricted areas, HazCom, lockout-tagout). Monday Week 2: Observation day on production floor/cleanroom. Tue–Fri Week 2: Lab training — simulated build/test exercises; workers tested throughout. Active participation is critical — workers who fail typically did not engage or ask questions. AMAT provides cleanroom attire (jumpsuit, hat, gloves, shoe covers, goggles). Worker must bring steel-toe shoes.
          52Shift confirmed in Fieldglass (shift assigned during Phase 6 matching)FieldglassAMATShift assignment
          Shift differentials: Day 0%, Swing 10%, Night 13%, Weekend Day 10%, Weekend Swing 18%, Weekend Night 23%. Logistics shifts: 12-hr A/B rotation. 18 Fieldglass OT/DT codes apply. All differentials are fixed after training.
          53🎉 Monday Week 3 — Worker moves to assigned production floor and shift. ONBOARDING COMPLETE.On-SiteAMAT Hiring LeaderWorker active on floor
          Phase 9 — Active Employment & EOR Responsibilities
          Fieldglass + Contractor App — Steps 54–60 — EOR Phase 4
          Steps 54–60
          Raise is the Employer of Record. Raise owns all payroll, statutory compliance, and employment lifecycle management. AMAT owns worksite conditions and performance management. TX launch uses Fieldglass only for time entry (NMO not required until CA sites go live).
          9a — Timekeeping & Payroll  —  Owner: Raise
          #ActionSystemOwnerDetail
          54Worker submits time in Fieldglass (end of week); AMAT stakeholder approves timesheetFieldglassWorker / AMATTimesheet; Approved timesheet
          No timeclocks — self-reported. Cutoff: Day/Swing/Night = Monday; Weekend = Sunday night. Auto-calculates OT. Manager approves; Raise does NOT approve; EWOps tracks. Workers follow Raise holiday policy — if AMAT is closed, workers do not report. Escalation: Srisalini Myneni (EW Ops).
          55CS reviews and validates timesheets; coordinates payroll. Invoice consolidated weekly (Wednesdays).Contractor AppCSValidated timesheets; payroll records; Wednesday invoice
          CS copies Payroll on weekly pulls. Shift differentials + OT stacking complexity — CS review before payroll catches issues early. Invoicing to EW_Ops@amat.com. AMAT self-invoices. Payment within 45 days (N45). 0.5% Fieldglass VMS fee added to Raise bill rate. Typical hours: 45–55/wk, occasionally 60. Texas FLSA: OT after 40 hrs at 1.5×. Invoice correction window: 60 days.
          ⚠ Open: OT calculated on base only or base + differential? Bill rate formula? How do 18 OT/DT rate codes map to pay calculations? Holiday pay + OT + differential interaction (holiday schedule to be provided by Pamela).
          9b — Worker Support & Contractor Success  —  Owner: Raise
          #ActionSystemOwnerDetail
          56Ongoing CS check-ins — satisfaction, issue resolution, retention. On-Site Manager walks facilities and liaises with AMAT VMO on upcoming demand.Contractor App / On-SiteCS / On-Site ManagerDedicated CS team: phone, email, in-app chat
          Worker issues (payroll, scheduling) → Raise. Worksite issues → AMAT EW Ops. Performance concerns: EWOps receives from managers, forwards coaching emails to Raise. Accommodations: Accommodations_Program@amat.com. All other escalations: EWOps team.
          9c — Safety & Incident Reporting  —  Owner: Raise (EOR) + AMAT (worksite)
          #ActionSystemOwnerDetail
          57Raise orientation: how to report injury/incident to Raise; timecard submission processContractor AppRaiseOrientation content
          58Incident reporting: worker notifies Raise → Raise supports AMAT VMO. AMAT notifies Raise of any worksite incident.Raise + AMATRaise (EOR) + AMATIncident SOP; WC claims (Raise carries)
          ⚠ Open: Post-accident drug testing policy for contingent workers? Random drug testing extended to contingent workers? Incident process on shifts when Raise is not on-site?
          9d — Assignment Changes, Extensions, Conversions & Offboarding  —  Owner: Raise + AMAT
          ItemDetail
          Max assignment18 months (US) • 90-day break in service required before re-engagement • 24 months (Singapore)
          FTE conversion rate50%+ of contractors convert to FTE
          Conversion fees0–30 days: 15%  |  31–60 days: 10%  |  61–90 days: 5%
          Notice periodsCE = 2 weeks  |  IC = 10 days
          Non-competeCE = N/A  |  IC = 90 days
          Non-solicitationCE = 90 days  |  IC = 180 days
          Training dropoutRaise aware → email EWOps immediately. AMAT aware → EWOps emails Raise.
          OffboardingRaise coordinates badge/equipment return, confirms system access removed. Workers ending in good standing assessed for redeployment. Exit interviews conducted.
          ⚠ Open: Operational process when worker approaches 18 months? Exception to 90-day break ever granted? Who initiates conversion notification to Raise? Notification process when assignment ends early? Rehire eligibility — told immediately or only when submitting again?
          9e — Worker Module & Re-Engagement  —  Owner: Curation / CS
          #ActionSystemOwnerDetail
          59Ongoing workforce management — worksite check-ins, performance escalations, AMAT VMO liaisonOn-Site / SFOn-Site ManagerFirst point of contact for workers on-site
          60Update Workforce Module as assignment changes; track 18-month tenure; set re-engagement nurturing campaign when worker nears 90-day break eligibilityFieldglass / SFCuration / CSFG profile update; re-engagement campaign
          Placed workers remain in Workforce Module flagged as ‘started/placed.’ Set automated SF notification to Curation when worker is within 30 days of 90-day break eligibility. Nurturing campaign re-engages workers coming off break period and surfaces them for new reqs.
          ✎My Notes
          Applied Materials

          Open Items & Risks

          Outstanding decisions and active risks. V2 — Updated April 20, 2026.

          ✓ RESOLVED 1. BGC Validity Period
          Valid for 6 months. Drug screen valid for 30 days.
          ✓ RESOLVED 2. Conditional Offer Timing
          Resolved by new sequencing: COI collected first, then conditional offer issued after COI clearance and before on-site assessment. BGC initiated after assessment pass with proper consent from conditional offer.
          ⚠ ACTIVE RISK 3. Drug Screen 30-Day Window
          Tight coordination required between pool management and req flow. Weekly volume ~30–45 roles across all suppliers. If drug screen expires, candidate should retest immediately or be pulled from module until retested. Set up automated Salesforce notifications for expiry tracking.
          📋 OPERATIONAL STEP 4. Candidate Duplication — Operational Step
          During recruitment screening, validate whether the candidate is already in another supplier's workforce pool. Flag that different bill rates across suppliers could impact placement. Being new to the site, building a strong network means some overlap is expected — AMAT will review Raise candidates first. This is an operational step, not a risk.
          ⏳ PENDING 5. COI Digital Signature Collection
          Salesforce does not currently support digital signatures easily. Follow up with Connor on customization options: e-signature integration (DocuSign/Adobe Sign), wet signature + scan, or in-person collection at assessment. Decision needed before go-live.
          ⏳ PENDING 6. Rehire Form Clarification
          Confirm whether the formal 'Rehire form' completed during in-onboarding is an AMAT document or a Raise document. Note that candidates who are 'rehires' to AMAT may be first-time Raise workers — the Raise employment relationship is separate.
          ⏳ PENDING 7. Export Compliance Certification
          Unknown whether the Export Compliance Cert (Exhibit A to the AMNA) is required for bulk hire roles. Flag for confirmation with AMAT before including in the in-onboarding checklist.
          ⏳ PENDING 8. BGC Authorization Disclosure
          Confirm that AMAT's authorization form can prescribe both AMAT and Raise as requesting the background check. Follow up with First Advantage on disclosure setup.
          🔜 FUTURE 9. NMO / Fieldglass for CA Sites
          Not relevant for TX launch. When CA sites come online, confirm whether Fieldglass can handle clock-in/clock-out detail with meal and rest break tracking. If not, plan for dual entry (FG + NMO) for CA workers. This is a blocking decision for the CA time-entry workflow design.
          ✎My Notes
          Applied Materials

          EOR Onboarding & Employment Workflow

          Austin Bulk Hiring Program — Stages 8–10 and Active Employment. Updated April 2026. For the full 50-step sequence see End-to-End Workflow.

          ✎ Page Content
          ✓ Saved
          How to read: Items highlighted are open questions pending confirmation. All others are confirmed.
          📋
          Phases
          Phase 3 & 4
          ⏰
          Order to Start
          ~5–7 days
          🎓
          Training
          8 days
          🔴Critical Deadlines — Wednesday Training Start
          Activation cutoffThursday before training week (Friday 10AM CST absolute latest)
          Photo submissionMonday — white background only
          Badge pickupTuesday — on-site with valid ID
          Training startWednesday — classroom begins
          👷Roles in Scope
          Manufacturing TechniciansEngineering TechniciansMaterial HandlersMaterial Coordinators
          Phase 3 — EOR Onboarding & Activation
          Resource order through first day of training
          Stages 8–10
          8
          Stage 8 — Resource Order & Supplier Activities
          Owner: Raise + AMAT
          ▼
          R
          Accept resource order in Fieldglass
          Raise accepts the resource order. Onboarding can begin while PO integration processes (4–6 hrs). No extra approval layers for repetitive roles within budget.
          Open Items
          • Forklift roles — must workers arrive certified, or does AMAT certify on-site? Pamela: AMAT conducts onsite PIT test + physical assessment.
          • PIV designation tied to certification? Pamela confirmed: No.
          • Role changes during 18-month assignment — how is Raise notified?
          R
          Confirm background check complete
          BG check already completed during pipeline build. Raise marks complete in Fieldglass checklist.
          Confirmed by Pamela
          Security team provides email update on results.
          R
          Execute Contingent Worker Agreement
          Raise executes the AMAT Contingent Worker Confidentiality Agreement. Covers: confidentiality, IP, inventions, code of conduct, export compliance, 18-month max. Due at resource order — NOT Workforce Module.
          Confirmed
          Uploaded to Fieldglass.
          R
          EOR employment onboarding
          Employment agreement, tax docs, payroll enrollment, I-9, and applicable benefits enrollment.
          R
          Submit worker photo for badging
          Due Monday before Tuesday badge day. Photo must be on a white background. Late photos = 2–3 hour wait on badge day.
          A
          AMAT Security — clear BG check & review COI
          Marks background check as cleared in system and reviews the COI (Conflict of Interest) form.
          A
          AMAT EW Ops activates resource order
          Once all activities are complete, EW Ops activates the resource order — triggering Workday integration and Contractor ID generation.
          📦Onboarding Document Package
          Contingent Worker AgreementRequired — via Fieldglass
          U.S. Export Compliance (Exhibit A)Required
          Applied Travel Rules (Exhibit B)Not required for EOR workers
          Raise employment documentsRequired — tax, payroll, I-9
          9
          Stage 9 — System Activation & Badging
          Owner: AMAT (system) + Raise (coordination)
          ▼
          Thu/Fri before training week
          All supplier activities complete
          Thursday preferred. Friday 10:00AM CST is absolute latest for Wednesday start.
          Monday
          System activation + photo deadline
          Workday integration runs at 3AM and 3PM CST, generating Contractor IDs. Raise submits photos — badges pre-printed.
          Tuesday
          Badge pickup on-site
          Workers present valid ID, receive badge, complete system access with AMAT IT. Each supplier gets a time slot. Raise rep should be present.
          Open Items
          • What if a worker misses Tuesday badge pickup? Backup slot?
          • How and when are time slots assigned?
          Wednesday
          Training begins
          Worker is cleared, badged, systems active. Classroom training begins.
          10
          Stage 10 — Training & Production Ramp
          Owner: AMAT (delivery) + Raise (support)
          ▼
          Training is paid. Day shift trains days; swing & night train during swing schedule.
          Wed–Fri, Week 1
          Classroom training
          Foundational knowledge, safety, systems. Safety covers: evacuation, PPE, restricted areas, HazCom, lockout-tagout.
          Monday, Week 2
          Observation day
          Workers observe the cleanroom environment on the production floor.
          Tue–Fri, Week 2
          Lab training
          Simulated build/test exercises. Workers tested throughout. Active participation is critical.
          Monday, Week 3
          Move to production floor
          Workers meet managers and move to assigned floor and shift.
          Open Items
          • Training hours — 8:00AM–5:00PM? (to be confirmed)
          Phase 4 — Active Employment: EOR Responsibilities
          Raise as EOR — payroll, compliance, worker support
          Raise EOR
          4a
          Timekeeping & Payroll
          Owner: Raise
          ▼
          ItemDetail
          Time entryFieldglass timesheet module — end of week. Auto-calculates OT.
          Cutoff (Day/Swing/Night)Monday
          Cutoff (Weekend)Sunday night
          ApprovalManager approves. Raise does NOT approve. EWOps tracks.
          TimeclocksNone — self-reported in Fieldglass
          Invoice correction60 days. Raise initiates → EWOps reviews → business approves
          InvoicingConsolidated weekly (Wednesdays). Payment within 45 days.
          VMS fee0.5% Fieldglass fee — added to Raise bill rate
          OvertimeTypically 45–55 hrs/week. Texas FLSA: OT after 40 hrs at 1.5x.
          Shift differentials0% (Day) to 23% (Weekend Night). Fixed after training.
          Pending
          • OT on base only or base + differential? Bill rate formula?
          • Nine OT rate codes — how do they map to pay calculations?
          • Holiday schedule (Pamela to provide)
          • Holiday pay + OT + differential interaction
          4b
          Worker Support & Contractor Success
          Owner: Raise
          ▼
          ItemDetail
          Contractor Success teamDedicated team trained on AMAT — phone, email, or in-app chat.
          Check-insRaise leads check-in calls. Updates to EWOps if resource needs support.
          Performance concernsEWOps receives from managers, forwards coaching emails to Raise.
          Worker issuesPayroll/scheduling → Raise. Worksite issues → AMAT EW Ops.
          Accommodations[email protected]
          All other escalationsEWOps team
          4c
          Safety & Incident Reporting
          Owner: Raise (EOR) + AMAT (worksite)
          ▼
          Raise carries workers' comp. AMAT owns worksite conditions and site-specific safety training.
          ItemDetail
          Raise orientationCovers: how to report injury/incident to Raise, how to submit timecards.
          Incident reportingWorker notifies Raise → Raise supports AMAT VMO. AMAT notifies Raise of any incident.
          EHS alignment callScheduled week of 4/20
          Open Items
          • Post-accident drug testing policy for contingent workers?
          • Random drug testing extended to contingent workers?
          • Incidents on shifts when Raise is not on-site?
          4d
          Assignment Changes, Extensions & Conversions
          Owner: Raise + AMAT
          ▼
          ItemDetail
          Max assignment18 months
          FTE conversion rate50%+ of contractors convert to FTE
          Conversion fees0–30 days: 15%  |  31–60 days: 10%  |  61–90 days: 5%
          Open Items
          • Operational process when worker approaches 18 months?
          • Exception to 3-month break ever granted?
          • Who initiates conversion notification to Raise?
          4e
          Offboarding
          Owner: Raise + AMAT
          ▼
          ItemDetail
          Training dropoutRaise aware → email EWOps immediately. AMAT aware → EWOps emails Raise.
          Asset returnRaise coordinates badge, equipment return. Confirms system access removed.
          RedeploymentWorkers ending in good standing assessed for redeployment. Exit interviews conducted.
          Open Items
          • Notification process when assignment ends early? Lead time?
          • Rehire eligibility — told immediately or only when submitting again?

          Applied Materials — Legal

          Contingent Worker Confidentiality Agreement

          Required for all workers. Executed by Raise, uploaded to Fieldglass at resource order stage.

          ✎ Page Content
          ✓ Saved
          📅
          When Due
          At resource order
          📤
          Delivery
          Fieldglass upload
          ⏳
          Max Period
          < 18 months
          Important: NOT required at Workforce Module stage — due when resource order is created. Raise executes with worker and provides copy to AMAT via Fieldglass.
          📋Parties & Key Fields
          Date___ day of __________, 20__
          Applied partyApplied Materials, Inc. and its subsidiaries ("Applied")
          Contingent WorkerWorker name — filled at signing
          AgencyRaise (as the employing agency)

          Click any section to expand

          1
          Confidential Information
          ▼

          Scope includes:

          • Technical: manufacturing techniques, processes, formulas, designs, source code, test results, know-how
          • Commercial: business plans, sales strategies, financial info, budgets, price lists
          • Personnel: org charts, salaries, performance reviews
          • Customer/Supplier: contact lists, product requirements, purchase histories

          Worker must: hold CI in trust; not disclose outside Applied; only use CI in performing services for Applied. On termination, promptly return all data, documents, and equipment.

          DTSA: Worker not liable for disclosing a trade secret to a government official/attorney to report a suspected law violation, or in a sealed court filing.

          2
          Disclosure to Courts or Government Agencies
          ▼

          If legally required to disclose CI, worker must immediately notify Applied in writing so Applied can assert objections in time.

          3
          Code of Conduct
          ▼

          Worker must comply with Applied's Standards of Business Conduct including: protection of CI, gifts and meals policy, anti-bribery and anti-corruption.

          4
          Inventions, Patents, Trade Secrets & Copyrights
          ▼

          Applied Inventions: All inventions, discoveries, and original works made during the assignment. Worker assigns all rights worldwide to Applied. All Applied Inventions are "works made for hire."

          Records: Worker must maintain written records — these remain Applied's property at all times.

          Exclusions: Pre-existing inventions developed on own time without Applied resources are excluded unless they relate to Applied's business.

          One-year presumption: Any invention disclosed within 1 year after assignment end is presumed conceived during the assignment.

          5
          Ownership of Documents
          ▼

          All drawings, specs, designs, and information furnished by Applied remain Applied's sole property. Worker must return all items upon termination or upon request.

          6
          Information of Former Employer
          ▼

          Worker shall not disclose or use any confidential information of former employers for Applied's benefit. No non-public documents from former employers on Applied premises without prior written consent.

          7
          Status of Contingent Worker
          ▼

          Worker is an employee of Raise, not Applied. Not eligible for: bonus, PTO, stock options, Applied workers' comp, disability, medical, 401(k), retirement, profit sharing.

          Completion of a temporary assignment does not imply future employment with Applied.

          8
          Insurance
          ▼

          No insurance — including workers' compensation — obtained by Applied on worker's behalf. Insurance is carried by Raise as EOR.

          9
          Data Protection, Privacy & Monitoring
          ▼

          Worker has no expectation of personal privacy on Applied systems (computers, email, internet, etc.). Applied can monitor, record, or search any resources at any time, with or without notice. Worker consents to Applied processing personal data for assignment purposes.

          10
          Maximum Service Period
          ▼

          If classified as a "temporary worker," the work assignment at Applied will be for a period of less than 18 months.

          11
          Activities & Awards
          ▼

          Contingent Workers are not eligible for company-sponsored social/business activities outside work hours or off-premises, nor for any Applied award or recognition programs.

          12
          Indemnification
          ▼

          Unauthorized use or disclosure of CI makes worker liable for all damages including attorneys' fees and court costs. Any breach causes irreparable harm — injunctive relief is an appropriate remedy.

          13
          Arbitration
          ▼

          All controversies settled by binding arbitration via JAMS or AAA. Claims must be filed within 1 year. Prevailing party may collect attorneys' fees. Does NOT apply to injunctive relief claims for unauthorized CI disclosure.

          14–16
          Entire Agreement · Severability · Obligations
          ▼

          14. This document + Exhibit A is the sole agreement. Modifications require written signature of both parties.

          15. If any provision is invalid, the remainder continues in full force.

          16. All obligations bind Worker's heirs, executors, and assigns.

          Exhibits

          A
          Exhibit A — U.S. Export Compliance Certification
          ▼
          Mandatory for all non-employees. Return with the Contingent Worker Confidentiality Agreement.

          Worker certifies compliance with U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR). Will not export technology, software, or commodities in violation of U.S. export control laws.

          Required Fields
          Legal NameLast · First · MI
          Non-Employee Worker #If available
          Hiring AgencyRaise
          Country of Citizenship/ResidencyList all

          High-restriction countries: China (PRC), Russia, Iran, North Korea, Cuba, Syria, Sudan, Belarus, Burma, Ukraine, and others. Application process significantly more burdensome for citizens/residents of these countries.

          B
          Exhibit B — State Invention Assignment Exclusions
          ▼
          Required attachment if worker is in CA, KS, IL, MN, or WA — or company may forfeit important rights.

          These state statutes exclude from mandatory assignment any invention developed entirely on worker's own time without employer resources, unless it relates to the employer's business or results from work performed for the employer.

          California
          Labor Code § 2870
          Washington
          RCW 49.44.140
          Illinois
          765 ILCS 1060/2
          Minnesota
          § 181.78
          Kansas
          § 44-130
          ✎My Notes
          Click below to add notes
          Applied Materials — Compliance

          Conflict of Interest Disclosure Form

          Completed by all candidates seeking placement as an extended worker at AMAT. Reviewed by AMAT Security.

          ✎ Page Content
          ✓ Saved
          📋Key Facts
          Who completesCandidate / extended worker
          Who reviewsAMAT Security team
          Delivery methodUploaded to Fieldglass by Raise
          When requiredBefore resource order activation
          1
          Personal Relationships at AMAT
          ▼

          Do you have a close personal relationship (familial or romantic) with any director, officer, employee or contractor of Applied Materials?

          If yes, describe: name, job title, relationship.

          2
          Outside Business Activities
          ▼

          Are you involved in any business activities outside of the potential contracted engagement with Applied Materials, such as consulting work, part-time employment, or ownership of an outside business?

          If yes, describe: business name, role, industry, overlap with Applied.

          3
          Outside Board or Advisory Positions
          ▼

          Do you have a position on any outside board or advisory group?

          If yes, describe: board/group name, role, industry, overlap with Applied.

          4
          Financial Interest in Competitors or Partners
          ▼

          Do you, or a member of your family, have a significant financial interest or leadership position in any entity that is a current or potential supplier, customer, business partner, or competitor of Applied Materials?

          If yes, describe: entity name, type of financial interest, public/private, relationship to Applied.

          5
          Personal Relationships at Competitors or Partners
          ▼

          Do you have a close personal relationship (familial or romantic) with someone working for a current or potential supplier, customer, business partner, or competitor of Applied Materials?

          If yes, describe: name, job title, relationship, name of relation's employer.

          ✎My Notes
          Click below to add notes
          Applied Materials — Compliance

          Background Check Adjudication Matrix

          Applied Materials contractor adjudication criteria. Based on disposition date only — last 7 years.

          ✎ Page Content
          ✓ Saved
          Key rule: Adjudication is based on disposition date only. Do NOT include sentences, probation periods, etc. Only include records from the last 7 years.
          🇺🇸North America — SSN Verification
          ✓ EligibleExact match for Name and SSN
          ⚠ ReviewSSN not issued; no results returned; SSN issued within last 5 years (if info matches)
          ✕ IneligibleName on trace is deceased; other names attached to SSN; SSN cannot be validated
          🌐North America — Global Sanctions
          ✓ EligibleNo matching records; clear record; no convictions; does not meet ineligible criteria
          ⚠ ReviewName match found and verified to be applicant
          ✕ IneligibleName match found but further research shows NOT the applicant
          🔍North America — Criminal Background (County, Statewide, National)
          ✓ EligibleNo record / clear / no convictions; dispositions: Not Guilty, Nolle Prosse, Dismissed, Expunged, Pardoned; marijuana misdemeanor 2+ years old
          ⚠ ReviewName match verified; restraining/stay away orders in public records; under arrest or indictment pending adjudication
          ✕ IneligibleAny felony conviction or pending felony • Any crime of violence (assault, battery, stalking, domestic violence) • Any sex-related offense • Any theft-related offense • Use/possession of deadly weapon or firearm • Multiple DUI/DWI (2+ within 7 years) • Narcotics possession w/ incarceration >30 days • Motor vehicle violation w/ fine >$2,500 or incarceration >30 days • Any undisclosed criminal conviction
          🇮🇳India — Global Sanctions
          ✓ EligibleNo matching record found
          ⚠ ReviewName match found and verified
          ✕ IneligibleName match found but NOT the applicant
          🇨🇦Canada — Global Sanctions
          ✓ EligibleNo matching record found
          ⚠ ReviewName match found and verified
          ✕ IneligibleName match found but NOT the applicant
          🎙️
          Recruiter Resource

          How to Explain the Background Check Matrix to Candidates

          Before you start: Only share what the candidate needs to know. Do not read the full ineligibility list verbatim — it can create unnecessary anxiety. Use the script below to set context, answer questions calmly, and direct escalations to your manager.
          1️⃣Opening — Setting Expectations

          "As part of the Applied Materials onboarding process, all contractors are required to complete a background check through our vendor, Checkr. This is a standard requirement for everyone — it's not specific to you or your background."

          "The check looks at a few things: identity verification using your Social Security Number, a global sanctions screen, and a criminal background search covering county, state, and national records. For placements in India or Canada, there's also a regional sanctions screen."

          "Applied Materials reviews results based on disposition date only, and only considers records from the last 7 years — so things like arrest dates or sentencing periods don't factor in, only when the case was actually closed."

          💡

          Tip: Saying "standard for everyone" upfront reduces the candidate's anxiety and prevents them from assuming they're being singled out.

          2️⃣Explaining the Three Possible Results

          "Once the check is submitted, there are three possible outcomes:"

          ✅

          Eligible — Clear to proceed

          "Everything came back clear — your identity matched, no sanctions hits, no disqualifying records. You're all set to continue onboarding."

          ⚠️

          Under Review — Needs a closer look

          "Your background check has flagged something that needs a manual review before a decision is made. This doesn't mean you're disqualified — it just means our compliance team needs to take a closer look. We'll follow up with you as soon as we have an update. This can take 1–3 business days."

          Common review triggers (do NOT share this full list with candidates): Name match on sanctions list, SSN issued within last 5 years, pending charges not yet adjudicated, restraining orders in public records.

          ❌

          Ineligible — Does not meet criteria

          "Unfortunately, the background check results indicate you don't meet the screening criteria required by Applied Materials for this role. We're not able to move forward with this placement. You will receive an adverse action notice from Checkr which includes the details and your rights."

          Do not specify which record caused the ineligibility — refer the candidate to the Checkr adverse action notice for details. Escalate to your manager before delivering this decision if unsure.

          3️⃣Common Candidate Questions & How to Answer Them
          ❓ "What exactly are you checking?"
          "We run an SSN trace to verify your identity, a global sanctions and watchlist check, and a criminal background check covering county, state, and national databases. For India and Canada placements, there's also a regional sanctions screen."
          ❓ "How far back does it go?"
          "Applied Materials only looks at the last 7 years, and they base it on the disposition date — meaning when the case was resolved, not when you were arrested or when a sentence ended."
          ❓ "I have something on my record — will I be disqualified?"

          "Not necessarily. The outcome depends on the nature and date of the record. Some things — like a Not Guilty finding, a dismissal, or certain older misdemeanors — may still result in a clear. If there's a concern, the compliance team will review it individually and reach out."

          Note for recruiters: Do not promise a specific outcome. Never tell a candidate their specific record will or won't be disqualifying — you don't make that determination. Let compliance handle it.

          ❓ "How long does it take?"
          "Most checks complete within 1–3 business days. If a manual review is needed, it may take a bit longer. We'll keep you updated."
          ❓ "Why do I have to do this? I passed a check at my last job."
          "Each client has their own screening requirements. Applied Materials has specific criteria that all contractors must meet, regardless of previous checks. It's a standard part of the onboarding process for this placement."
          ❓ "I want to dispute the results."
          "Absolutely — that's your right. Checkr's adverse action notice will explain the dispute process and your rights under the Fair Credit Reporting Act. You can contact Checkr directly to start that process. In the meantime, I'd recommend looping in your Contractor Success team member so we can note the dispute on our end."
          4️⃣When to Escalate

          Escalate to your manager or compliance team immediately if:

          → The candidate is pushing back aggressively on an ineligible result
          → The candidate has disclosed a record and is asking whether it will disqualify them
          → The check has been pending for more than 5 business days with no update
          → The candidate is threatening legal action or mentions an attorney
          → The result came back as Ineligible and you're unsure how to deliver the news
          ⚠️

          Never speculate on whether a specific record will or won't disqualify a candidate. That determination is made by the compliance team, not the recruiter.

          📋 Quick Cheat Sheet — What You CAN and CANNOT Share

          ✅ You CAN say

          • •What types of screens are included
          • •That the lookback period is 7 years
          • •That adjudication is based on disposition date
          • •That a review result is not the same as ineligible
          • •That the candidate can dispute via Checkr
          • •Typical processing time (1–3 business days)

          ❌ You CANNOT say

          • •Which specific record triggered the result
          • •Whether a specific record will/won't disqualify
          • •The full ineligibility criteria list
          • •Any guarantee about the outcome
          • •That a past check elsewhere will be accepted
          • •Legal advice of any kind
          ✎My Notes
          Click below to add notes
          RBC

          Onboarding in Fieldglass

          Step-by-step Fieldglass onboarding process for RBC — emails, background checks, placement updates, and tracker details.

          📧Step 1 — Three Onboarding Emails

          When onboarding begins, three emails are sent to the candidate:

          1️⃣

          Welcome email

          2️⃣

          Onboarding email (pre-hire)

          3️⃣

          Background check email

          🔍Step 2 — Initiate Background Check (Sterling Backcheck)
          Provider: Sterling Backcheck — used for all banking and motors (US accounts use either Karma Check or Checker)
          1

          Order in e-consent

          2

          Add all their information

          3

          Location — it's always Raise Limited

          4

          Select package — Number 3 Canada package

          5

          Submit

          📂Step 3 — Go to Placement & Update the Onboarding Tracker
          Go to Onboarding within the placement
          Update the tracker for background check → set to Initiated
          Add the BGC provider

          Mark the following checks as created:

          Criminal check has been created
          Credit check has been created
          Employment has been created
          Education has been created
          Add a note in the comments section
          ✅Step 4 — Update Onboarding Tracker Details
          Add portal — always use RC app — and the date we sent the invite
          Onboarding status — pick Background Checks and Credentials, then hit Save
          📋 Quick Reference — Full Sequence
          1. Send 3 emails: Welcome → Onboarding (pre-hire) → Background check
          2. Open Sterling Backcheck → Order e-consent → Fill in candidate info → Location = Raise Limited → Select Package #3 Canada → Submit
          3. Go to Placement → Onboarding → Update BGC tracker to Initiated → Add BGC provider → Mark Criminal / Credit / Employment / Education as created → Add comment
          4. Update tracker details → Portal = RC app + date invite sent → Onboarding status = Background Checks + Credentials → Save
          ✎My Notes
          Click below to add notes
          RBC

          CWM Follow-Up Process

          What to do when the CWM Program Office sends a CIS request email after a placement. Covers the CIS file, background check review, onboarding tracker updates, and candidate follow-up.

          When this applies: After a placement is made, the CWM Program Office (cwmprogoffice@rbc.cc) will send an email requesting a CIS file and candidate details. You need to respond with the completed CIS Excel file and relevant info. Sometimes this email arrives before onboarding has started — in that case, reply letting them know you're waiting for basic information from the candidate.
          📧Step 1 — Respond to the CWM Email
          Email body:
          "Please see attached CIS and details below for [CANDIDATE NAME]."
          Then fill in the Phone, Email, and Address fields the CWM email is asking for.
          Where to get the candidate details: Go to the Placement tab in Bullhorn → grab their Phone, Email, and Address.
          Work permit note: If the candidate has an expiring or already-expired work permit, include that in the email. If still valid, state: "The candidate's work permit is still valid up to [DATE]."
          📊Step 2 — Complete the CIS Excel File

          Open the CIS document (MS Excel). Fill in every field exactly as specified:

          FieldFormat / Rule
          Last NameALL CAPS — legal last name
          Given Name(s)All legal first names, lower case
          Date of BirthYYYY-MM-DD
          Position TitleExactly as listed on the assignment offer — go to Placement tab to locate the job title
          Phone #XXX-XXX-XXXX
          Email Addressusername @ domain name
          Civic AddressFull address
          Save as: copy of CIS CBS Check FIRSTNAME LASTNAME
          Then attach the saved Excel file to the reply email.
          🔍Step 3 — Review the Background Check (Sterling Backcheck)

          Go to Sterling Backcheck and check the candidate's file status. Look for anything pending or flagged.

          ✓ All clear
          No action needed on the BGC. Note it in your reply to CWM if relevant.
          ⚠ Something pending
          Mention it in the CWM reply email and reach out to the candidate to let them know what's missing.
          ID checks — verify: Candidate's name, date of birth, and whether the ID is expired.
          If a work permit is not valid: notify the client and check with the candidate first — ask if they have reapplied. If yes, request proof of reapplication.
          📂Step 4 — Update the Onboarding Tracker in Placement

          Go back to the Placement tab and update the candidate's onboarding tracker.

          Update BGC statuses — e.g. BGC Initiated, Criminal Record Clear, etc.
          Add a comment — note if the candidate is pending something and confirm that you followed up with them.
          Check which documents the candidate has completed and update the tracker accordingly.
          Emergency contact check: Make sure the emergency contact is a different person — not the candidate themselves.
          👤Step 5 — Candidate Tab: Create a Case & Send Follow-Up Email
          Go to the Candidate tab → Create a Case
          Name it something like: "Background Check Follow-Up" or whatever the specific follow-up item is
          Send the candidate an email letting them know what they are missing and what is currently happening with their file.
          📋 Quick Reference — Full CWM Follow-Up Sequence
          1. Reply to CWM email → body: "Please see attached CIS and details below for [NAME]." → get Phone, Email, Address from Placement tab → include work permit status
          2. Fill in CIS Excel → Last name CAPS, first name lower case, DOB as YYYY-MM-DD, position title from Placement tab, phone as XXX-XXX-XXXX, email, full address → Save as copy of CIS CBS Check FIRST LAST → attach to email
          3. Check Sterling Backcheck → If anything pending: mention in CWM reply + email candidate with what's missing → Verify IDs: name, DOB, expiry. Work permit invalid? Notify client + ask candidate for proof of reapplication
          4. Placement tab → Update onboarding tracker (BGC Initiated, Criminal Clear, etc.) → Add comment → Check completed docs → Verify emergency contact is not the candidate themselves
          5. Candidate tab → Create a Case → Send follow-up email to candidate about anything missing

          📌 Important Notes

          • If the CWM email arrives before onboarding has started, reply letting them know you are waiting to get basic information from the candidate.
          • CWM will send the start date — then reach out to the candidate, and once they confirm, send out the contract.
          ✎My Notes
          Click below to add notes
          RBC

          Sending Onboarding — CE

          Step-by-step process for sending onboarding to a new Contract Employee placement at RBC Canada.

          📧Step 1 — Send Three Onboarding Emails
          1.Welcome email
          2.Onboarding email + packages — RBC Pre-Hire package AND RBC CAN package
          3.Background check email
          🔍Step 2 — Background Check (Sterling Backcheck)
          Sterling Backcheck is used for all banking and motors clients. US accounts use either KarmaCheck or Checkr.
          1.Order in e-consent
          2.Add all candidate information
          3.Location: always Raise Limited
          4.Select package — Number 3 Canada package
          5.Submit
          📂Step 3 — Update Placement Onboarding Tracker

          Go to Placement → Onboarding tab

          1.Update tracker — BGC status to Initiated
          2.Add the BGC provider
          3.Mark created: Criminal check, Credit check, Employment, Education
          4.Add a note in the comments section
          5.Portal: always use RC App — add the date the invite was sent
          6.Onboarding status → pick Background Checks and Credentials → hit Save
          ✎My Notes
          RBC

          Sending Onboarding — IC

          Onboarding process for Independent Consultant (IC) placements at RBC.

          Important: The AM (Boris) will typically trigger the portal invite and send the offer letter himself. If the recruiter does it, you'll need to trigger those steps instead.
          📋Step 1 — Contact Tab
          Check Onboarding Workflow phase — add IC Standard
          Select Onboarding Flow Part 1
          📤Step 2 — Placement Tab
          1.Send RBC Pre-Hire package
          2.Create a case → subject: Welcome → send welcome email → mark status Solved/Complete
          3.Create a case Orientation → send orientation email → mark status Solved/Complete
          4.Check if candidate needs an international background check (must have been in Canada for 5+ years). If needed, find the recruiter and send them a message.
          5.Create a case Background Check → send background check email
          📧Three Onboarding Emails
          1. Welcome email
          2. Onboarding/training email (pre-hire)
          3. Background check email
          🔍Background Check (Sterling Backcheck)
          Order in e-consent → add all candidate info → Location: Raise Limited → Package: #3 Canada → Submit
          📂Update Placement Onboarding Tracker
          BGC → Initiated · Add BGC provider · Mark Criminal, Credit, Employment, Education created · Add comment
          Portal: RC App + date sent · Onboarding status → Background Checks and Credentials → Save
          ✎My Notes
          CIBC

          New Placement — Incorporated (IC)

          Full onboarding workflow for a new IC placement at CIBC.

          👤Step 1 — Candidate Tab
          You will get an email for a placement case
          Activate Portal → Create new user → RC App
          📋Step 2 — Contact Page
          Make sure Onboarding Workflow and Onboarding Flow Part are selected
          📤Step 3 — Back to Candidate: Onboarding
          1.Go to Onboarding → Send CIBC Packet (if rehire: click "Submitted Placement")
          2.Package: CIBC Pre-Hire package → Send for signatures
          3.Create a case → Subject: Welcome email · → Send welcome email → All Lightning Templates → CIBC Templates → CIBC Welcome Email (Candidate Type e.g. IC) → Send → Mark Solved
          4.Create a case Orientation → Send: Orientation CIBC → Mark Solved
          ⚠Two critical items to send to the AM (with candidate's birthdate): Consent for Personal Information AND Contingent Labor Worker Agreement 2024
          🔍Step 4 — Background Check (Sterling Backcheck)
          1.Order in e-consent → add all candidate info
          2.Template: Invitation email from Raise · Location: Raise Limited
          3.Package: 5 years employment · Canadian criminal record check with electronic ID and local police · Education verification · Global sanctions and PEP · SIN Validation
          ⚠If candidate has not been in Canada for the last 5 years → need an international check. Reach out to the recruiter to clarify if unsure.
          4.Submit
          📂Step 5 — Update Placement Onboarding Tracker
          Placement → Onboarding tab/OB Tracker
          BGC → Initiated · Add BGC provider · Add date BGC ordered · Mark Criminal, Credit, Employment, Education created · Add comment
          Portal: RC App + date invite sent · Onboarding status → Background Checks and Credentials → Save
          ✎My Notes
          CIBC

          Rehire Process

          Onboarding workflow for a CIBC rehire — same steps as new placement with a few key differences.

          Key rehire rules: Use one profile only — do not create a new profile for a rehire. Make sure to update the onboarding workflow in the Contact tab.
          📋Step 1 — Contact Page
          Make sure Onboarding Workflow and Onboarding Flow Part are selected
          Update the onboarding workflow in the Contact tab
          We need to use just one profile if they are a rehire
          👤Step 2 — Candidate Tab
          You will get an email for a placement case
          Activate Portal → Create new user → RC App
          📤Step 3 — Back to Candidate: Onboarding
          1.Send CIBC Packet (pre-hire) → click "Submitted Placement" for rehires
          2.Package: CIBC Pre-Hire package → Send for signatures
          3.Create a case → Subject: Welcome email · → Send welcome email → All Lightning Templates → CIBC Templates → CIBC Welcome Email (Candidate Type) → Send → Mark Solved
          4.Create a case Orientation → Send: Orientation CIBC → Mark Solved
          ⚠Send Consent for Personal Information + Contingent Labor Worker Agreement 2024 to the AM with the candidate's birthdate
          🔍Step 4 — Background Check (Sterling Backcheck)
          e-consent → all candidate info → Template: Invitation email from Raise → Location: Raise Limited
          Package: 5 years employment · Canadian criminal record check (electronic ID + local police) · Education verification · Global sanctions & PEP · SIN Validation
          Not in Canada 5+ years? → International check required. Reach out to the recruiter to clarify.
          Submit
          📂Step 5 — Update Placement Onboarding Tracker
          BGC → Initiated · Add BGC provider · Add date BGC ordered · Mark Criminal, Credit, Employment, Education created · Add comment
          Portal: RC App + date invite sent · Onboarding status → Background Checks and Credentials → Save
          ✎My Notes
          QXO

          Onboarding Workflow

          Step-by-step onboarding process for QXO placements (US W2).

          First: Always check the onboarding workflow before starting.
          📂Step 1 — Placement Tab
          Input Assignee Type → W2-STATE
          Input Start Date and Scheduled End
          👤Step 2 — Candidate Profile
          1.Send placement
          2.Packages → US W2 NON CA
          3.Send forms → QXO Agreement → Send each document to sign
          4.Create a case → send welcome email (manual send)
          5.Send offer letter (W2)
          6.Create a case → send Equipment Return Policy
          🏋️Step 3 — Send Training (Manual)
          Only if assignee type wasn't added when placement was made
          Training modules: Before you start · Health and Safety · Harassment
          Choose Raise, then enter address for the job → Choose Raise Standard
          🔍Step 4 — Background Check (Checkr)
          1.Go to Checkr — candidate will receive the link
          2.Fill in candidate information
          3.Order Light Industrial package
          ✎My Notes
          QXO

          Contract Stop

          Process for ending a contractor's assignment.

          🛑Contract Stop Process
          ⚠If the candidate does not work on weekends, wait for the contractor to end, then check the last timesheet and use that as their end date
          ⚠If the AM doesn't put an end date in the change request — we cannot work on it
          1.Fill out Last Working Date
          2.Fill out Contract End Code
          3.Change Placement Status from Approved → Completed
          4.Go to Candidate → Create new GP → send email
          ✎My Notes
          QXO

          Contract Change — W2

          Process when a candidate is extended, or their end date changes.

          Use this when a candidate will be extended or their end date will be changed. You can find extension details in Case → Request Notes.
          📋Step 1 — Update the Case / Request
          Start date: when we are going to get a new contract
          End date: change to the new extended date
          Request status → change to Approved → click Save
          🔗Step 2 — Placement Tab
          Create new GP integration
          👤Step 3 — Candidate Profile
          1.Send placement package → APPROVED
          2.Send new contract in onboarding tab → Forms: Raise W2 Offer Letter → Create a request → Send signature → Complete the document
          3.Check all boxes inside the offer letter → Add signature → Follow prompts to sign
          4.Create a case → Manual contract extension for signing → Email candidate that new contract has been sent → Click Solved
          ✎My Notes
          Thales

          Onboarding Workflow

          Step-by-step onboarding for Thales DIS USA placements.

          👤Step 1 — Candidate Tab
          1.You will get an email for a placement case
          2.Activate Portal → Create new user → RC App
          3.Go to Onboarding → Placement click → Packages: Thales DIS USA → Send signature request
          4.Create a case → Subject: Welcome email · → Send welcome email → Insert template → Thales US → Thales US - Welcome Email (W2) → Send → Mark Complete
          ⚠There is no specific orientation email for Thales
          🔍Step 2 — Background Check (KarmaCheck)
          1.Go to KarmaCheck → Order a new report
          2.Candidate → fill in details: Email, Name, Contact information, Billing identifier (Raise)
          3.Choose package → Thales US
          4.Only keep eCup+ 10 — remove all other drug screenings
          5.Send invite
          ✎My Notes
          Lyft

          Program Overview

          Key contacts, activation model, staffing structure, and program rules for the Lyft Brand Ambassador program.

          ⚠ Single POC Rule: Lyft wants only one point of contact from our side. Marci is the POC for all Lyft communications.
          👤Client Contacts
          NameTitleScope
          MarciOur POCSingle point of contact on our side for all Lyft communications
          MeganProgram ManagerManaging the Brand Ambassadors — primary client POC. Sends COI copies. Manages Brand Ambassador tracker (hours, headcount, bill).
          Tina RaulsPMHandles contracts
          Chase SmithManagerManages events, airports, venues, and themes
          Amanda ChasinManagerSupports all around
          🎪Activation Model
          What is an activation? Robust lounges set up to support patrons and writers for app support and customer service. Typically a 3-day period with 12–14 hour shifts (with breaks in between). Leads are the main POC on site and manage the parameters of the activation.
          Duration: Some working days can be 10 days or up to 24. The events can change depending on needs — they've had 5+ days with 10 staff and 3 leads.
          First event: Around June 8th or 10th. 2 more events coming up in June.
          Staffing per airport: 1 Lead + 2 BAs per airport. Airport list (including specific dates, needs, hours, pay per location) will be provided by the client.
          Geography: Supporting US and Canada.
          🙋Candidate Profile — Brand Ambassadors
          Orientation: Customer service focused · Preppy appearance
          Age: Age plays a factor in selection
          Physical: Some light lifting and moving around — must be able to stand and walk for extended hours
          Leads: Raise will interview lead roles
          📋Program Rules & Admin
          COI required per event: Each event requires a Certificate of Insurance (COI) for BAs. A copy will be sent by Megan.
          BA Tracker: Managed by Megan — includes hours, headcount, and bill.
          BGC validity: Valid for 6 months from the date it was ordered.
          BGC failures: Criminal background check is 7-year county — no adjudication matrix. If a candidate fails, they are not adjudicated — do not proceed with the candidate.
          ✎My Notes
          Lyft

          Background Check Matrix

          BGC rules, validity, and process for Brand Ambassador placements at Lyft.

          ⚠ Critical Rule — No Adjudication

          There is no adjudication matrix for Lyft. If a candidate fails the background check, do not adjudicate — they are immediately ineligible. Do not proceed with the candidate.

          🔍Background Check Requirements
          FieldDetail
          Check Type Criminal Background Check
          Scope 7-year county criminal history
          Adjudication Matrix ✕ None — Fail = Ineligible, no review
          Validity Period 6 months from the date the BGC was ordered
          Who is screened All Brand Ambassadors and Leads
          Geography US and Canada placements
          Failure outcome Candidate is immediately disqualified — no adjudication, no appeal, no proceed
          🔀Pass / Fail Decision

          ✅

          BGC Passes

          Candidate is eligible. Proceed with placement. BGC valid for 6 months from order date.

          ❌

          BGC Fails

          Stop. Candidate is ineligible. No adjudication. No review. Do not proceed.

          📅BGC Validity Tracking
          Validity: 6 months from the date the BGC was ordered (not from when it was cleared). Track the order date carefully — if a candidate is reused for a future event beyond 6 months, a new BGC must be ordered.
          If a candidate is being reused across multiple events, confirm the BGC order date before assigning. A BGC ordered for a June event would be valid through approximately December of the same year.
          📋 Quick Reference — Lyft BGC Rules
          •Check type: 7-year county criminal check
          •Valid for: 6 months from order date
          •No adjudication matrix — fail = done, no exceptions
          •Applies to: all BAs and Leads, US and Canada
          •COI required per event — copy sent by Megan
          •Raise interviews Lead roles
          ✎My Notes

          Editable offer letter templates. Click any template to edit. Use the + button to add a new template for a different role.
