          ✎My Notes
          Lyft — Workwell MSP

          Onboarding Requirements

          Background investigations, onboarding documents, offer process, extensions, and offboarding. Source: Workwell MSP Supplier Operations Guide 2026.

          No worker may begin work without full completion of all onboarding requirements and prior written consent from Workwell’s Program Manager. Failure to comply may result in suspension or removal from the program.
          📋Offer & Onboarding

          Offer Out

          • Offer email + Talient offer with tentative start date sent
          • Start dates given 7–10 days out to allow time for BGC and onboarding
          • Let Workwell know of any onboarding delays
          • Cleared BGCs required before worker starts
          • Required onboarding docs are in Talient upon offer acceptance — download blank forms from there
          • Onboarding docs + BGC must be submitted into Talient by Monday EOD to start the following Monday

          Rejections

          • Notify MSP team immediately if a candidate rejects an offer
          • Try to provide rejection details
          • Was it pay related? Did the candidate have other offers?
          🕵Background Investigations
          BGC is at Supplier’s cost. Workwell/Lyft reserve the right to modify requirements immediately upon notice. Supplier must confirm adherence in writing upon request.

          US-Based Workers — Required Checks:

          Check TypeRequirement
          County CriminalPast 7 years
          Federal CriminalPast 7 years
          Criminal Enhanced NationwideRequired for all
          DMV / MVROnly for roles requiring driving (see driving requirements below)
          Healthcare ScreeningOnly for roles servicing Medicare/Medicaid — monthly basis

          Driving Requirements (driving roles only):

          • No more than 2 non-moving violations in past 3 years
          • Must currently be privileged to drive a passenger vehicle or motorcycle
          • No more than 2 accidents in past 3 years
          • No moving violations and no non-moving violation in past 3 years (for some roles)
          • No more than 1 moving violation in past 3 years
          • No major violation in past 5 years
          • Driver must be over 18 years old

          Healthcare Screening (monthly, applicable roles only):

          • OIG LEIE (HHS Office of Inspector General)
          • SAM.gov Exclusion List
          • All available State Medicaid Exclusion Database Sources
          Notify Program Manager if candidate does not clear all BGC requirements. Lyft reserves the right to reject any placement that does not meet adjudication guidelines.
          📄Submitting BGC Results in Talient
          1. Log in to Talient™
          2. Navigate to Workers tab → select desired worker in onboarding status
          3. Under the onboarding tracker, download the background checklist and complete each section
          4. Upload the completed checklist — DO NOT upload the full background investigation report
          5. Notification of approval issued by Talient™ after Program Manager review
          📝Onboarding Documents
          • All contingent workers must sign Lyft onboarding docs before starting assignment
          • Download docs from the Onboarding section of the worker’s profile in Talient (click blue text items)
          • Always use Talient docs to ensure most up-to-date versions
          • E-signature is accepted
          • Upload executed documents under Completed Document section
          • Approval notification issued by Talient after Program Manager review
          • Deadline: Monday EOD for worker to start the following Monday

          Extensions

          • Workers typically extended 3–6 months at a time
          • You’ll receive a Talient notification when your worker’s extension is approved and updated

          Offboarding

          • Program team sends offboarding notices via email
          • Lyft IT sends a QR code to the worker’s personal email address for laptop returns
          ✎My Notes
          Lyft — Workwell MSP

          Timekeeping & Expenses

          Talient timekeeping deadlines, time entry, timesheet edits, exceptions, adjustments, and expense submission. Source: Workwell MSP Supplier Operations Guide 2026.

          🚨 Time Entry & Weekly Deadlines

          Time Entry

          • Contractors access Talient via Okta SSO tile on their app dashboard
          • Timesheets are Monday–Sunday weekly for US
          • Canada timesheets are Sunday–Saturday and capture 2 weeks
          • US timesheets available on Mondays; Canada on Sundays
          • Both US and Canada timesheets due by Sunday at midnight
          • Managers asked to approve on Monday
          • Final deadline: Tuesday at noon — approved time pulled for invoicing

          Timesheet Exceptions

          • Timesheet adjustments due within 45 days
          • Taxes for Canada due within 45 days of work week — billed via Misc Invoice
          • MSP Fees are not billed on Expenses
          💻Worker Talient Access
          DO NOT click “send invite.” Workers access Talient via Okta (Lyft’s SSO platform). Workers’ @lyft.com email address is used for SSO and system notifications.

          Provide access and timekeeping instructions to workers only after Workwell Program Manager has approved all onboarding requirements and worker is cleared to start.

          ⏰How to Enter Time (Worker Instructions)
          1. Log in to Talient™ via Okta
          2. From dashboard, click “Add Punch”
          3. Manually enter exact start time, lunch start, lunch end, and shift end time
          4. Hit Enter on keyboard to save — hours calculated and displayed after save
          Workers certify that hours entered accurately represent total hours worked, that meal/rest breaks were taken, and that any discrepancies will be reported immediately.
          ✎How to Edit Timesheets
          1. Click on the Time & Expense tab
          2. To add hours: click “Add punch”, enter missed punch(es), click “Save”
          3. To edit existing hours: click into the existing punch, edit, click “Save”
          4. To delete hours on historical timesheet: click into existing punch, click “Delete” — warning message will confirm before deleting
          ⚠Timesheet Exception (e.g. Sick Time, Holiday Pay)
          1. Log in to Talient™ → click Time & Expense tab
          2. Click “Add other”
          3. Enter number of units; select exception type from type-ahead list (e.g. Holiday Pay HP)
          4. For Sick Time: select “Pay only, do not bill”
          5. Click “Save” — Hiring Manager receives notification to approve
          🔄Timesheet Adjustments (Historical)
          Suppliers have 45 days from date services were performed to submit a timesheet adjustment. Adjustments are routed to hiring manager for approval, then captured in next invoicing cycle.
          1. Log in to Talient™
          2. Click Workers → select desired worker
          3. In Time & Expense section, select desired week ending
          4. Click “Add Punch” to add or remove time from existing punch
          5. Once all adjustments added for one week ending, ensure Invoice box is checked
          6. Click “Submit Adjustment” for approval
          💰How to Submit Expenses
          Expense items = non-hours transactions (phone, travel, equipment). Pass-through — no MSP/VMS fee. Worker submits expense first; supplier then adds the pay code within 2 business days.

          Supplier steps after worker submits expense:

          1. Click Workers tab → select desired worker
          2. In Expenses & Other section, click “Add new”
          3. Enter the Pay Code (see table below)
          4. Leave Mark up override blank
          5. Enter Job title from dropdown
          6. Enter Date of Expense
          7. Enter Description
          8. Enter Amount
          9. Leave “Pay only, do not bill” BLANK (expenses must be billed)
          10. Attach backup documentation
          11. Click Save

          Pay Codes:

          Pay CodeDescription
          BBBonus
          HBBonus Holiday
          RBBonus Referral
          BBNNon-Discretionary Bonus
          OBNon-Discretionary Bonus OT Premium
          ReimCPReimburse Cell Phone
          ReimEQReimburse Equipment
          ReimLGReimburse Lodging
          ReimMLReimburse Meal
          RTReimbursement Taxable
          ReimORReimburse Other (misc.)
          Reim ANReimburse Transportation Non-Taxable
          RPRetro Pay
          SHShift Differential
          STSpecial Taxable
          SPStipend
          ✎My Notes
          Lyft — Workwell MSP

          Invoicing & Payments

          Weekly invoicing, supplier markups, misc invoice items, sales tax, pay file reports, and direct hire invoicing.

          💰Invoicing & Payments
          • Workwell invoices Lyft weekly for time & expenses approved in Talient by Tuesdays at noon
          • Suppliers do not submit invoices directly to Lyft
          • Workwell AP emails suppliers a Pay File and Punch Detail Report with all approved timesheets, expenses, and misc invoiced that week
          • Remittance details emailed by Workwell AP after payment
          • Workwell pays supplier within 17 days of receiving payment from Lyft
          • Lyft pays Workwell in 45 days — total payment time = NET62 for suppliers
          • Direct all invoicing inquiries to: emsp.invoice@workwellna.com
          • No MSP or VMS fees deducted from invoice (Lyft-funded program)
          Supplier invoices available Fridays in Talient under the Invoices Tab. Notifications sent via Talient on Fridays.
          📈Supplier Mark-Ups (US & Canada)
          Vertical (Job Category)Supplier Markup %
          IT / Engineering / Technical40%
          Creative40%
          Professional40%
          Field-Operations (Tech)45%
          Field-Operations (Non-Tech)43%

          Additional Pricing Notes

          • Mark-ups include MSP/VMS fee
          • 2.5% MSP/VMS fee deducted from supplier payments upon remittance
          • Supplier markups reduced by 10% after contractor reaches 2,080 continuous hours worked
          • Refer to contract for conversion terms
          • Audits are annual for BGC, onboarding docs & pay rates
          📋Miscellaneous Invoice Items
          For non-timesheet transactions (conversion fee, direct hire fee, rate adjustments, etc.). Suppliers have 45 days from date services were performed. Enter by EOD Wednesday; approvals needed by noon PST Thursday for Friday invoices.

          Steps:

          1. Click Clients → select Lyft
          2. Navigate to “Misc. Invoice Items” tab → click “Add misc. invoice item”
          3. Enter description using pipe-delimited format (see below)
          4. Enter Invoice Item Amount and Purchase Order # if applicable
          5. Upload approval documentation
          6. Click “Select Worker job” → complete form → click “Select”

          Description Format (pipe-delimited):

          Worker: Rob Smith | WE: 08-07-22 | Pay Code: RG | Supplier: Workwell Recruitment | Mgr: Jane Doe | Dept: Staffing | CC: 123 | Type: Credit to invoice 55443

          Pipe key on keyboard: Shift + \ (backslash key). Leave CC blank if not applicable, but keep the pipe: CC: |

          📄Sales Tax

          Submit as Misc. Invoice Item for workers in applicable states: CT, DC, HI, IA, NM, OH, PA, SD, WV. Submit before Friday for previous week’s hours. Submit weekly.

          Item: Worker Name | WE: 12.30.22 | Pay Code: Sales tax | Supplier: Workwell | Mgr: Mgr Name | CC: | Type: Sales tax
          📊Pay File & Punch Detail Report

          Talient issues weekly Pay File and Punch Detail Report once all hours are approved. Notification sent Tuesdays after 2:00 PM PST.

          To access:

          1. Log in to Talient™
          2. Click Clients → select Lyft
          3. Click Payrolling tab
          4. Select Pay File and/or PDR for desired pay week ending
          ReportDescription
          Payroll File (CSV)Excel report of all RG, OT, & DT processed
          Backup File (PDR)PDF report of all punches & exceptions processed per worker
          Payroll File (OTHER)Excel report of all approved exceptions processed
          🔗Direct Hire Invoicing
          Submit weekly through a Misc. Invoice Item. Must include approval documentation — failure to attach may delay approval.

          Description must include:

          Worker: [Name] | Title: [Title] | Hiring Manager: [Name] | Fee Amount: $X | Start Date: [Date]

          Leave Purchase Order blank. Upload approval documentation. No need to select a worker job.

          ✎My Notes
          Lyft — Workwell MSP

          Talient VMS Guide

          Requisition management, candidate submission, interview coordination, offer acceptance/rejection, and worker management in Talient.

          Talient™ is the sole system of record for the Lyft contingent worker program. No other systems are to be used. Circumventing Talient may result in suspension or removal from the program. All supplier users receive email notifications when a req is released.
          👥Program Support Team
          NameTitleContact
          Tina RaulsSenior Program Managertrauls@lyft.com
          Megan CampbellProgram ManagerMegancampbell@lyft.com
          LeRoi BarrettProgram Specialistlbarrett@lyft.com
          Supplier EngagementNew users / relationship questionsSuppliers@workwellna.com
          AP/AR Specialist TeamInvoicing, billing, paymentsemsp.invoice@workwellna.com
          📄Managing Requisitions & Candidates
          Add a Candidate to a Requisition
          1. Click Requisitions tab → select desired requisition
          2. Click “Add candidate” and complete all required fields
          3. In the Name section, select candidate from type-ahead list
          4. If candidate doesn’t populate: click “+” to add new worker — do not send invite yet
          5. Upload candidate document(s) by dragging or clicking into Documents section
          6. Add candidate interview availability
          7. Click “Save and present”
          Answer a Question on a Requisition
          1. You’ll receive email notification if hiring manager asks a question about your candidate
          2. Select requisition with unanswered question from Dashboard or Requisitions tab
          3. Click the number in the Questions column
          4. Provide response in the text box → press Enter to save
          Remove a Candidate
          1. Click Requisitions tab → select desired requisition → select worker
          2. Click “Remove candidate”
          Accept or Reject an Offer
          1. Click Workers tab → filter to show workers with “Offer Received”
          2. Select desired worker
          3. To accept: click “Accept offer” → begin onboarding process
          4. To reject: click “Reject offer” → Lyft notified automatically
          Interview Coordination

          When a hiring manager wants to schedule an interview, the Program Team will email to coordinate booking via Google Calendar booking links.

          Update a Worker
          1. Click Workers tab → select desired worker
          2. Click the edit icon next to the worker’s name
          3. Make desired updates → click “Save Worker”
          📊Worker Reports & Timesheet Viewing

          Worker Reports:

          1. Click Workers tab → use drop-down to filter worker status
          2. Add filters to refine worker list
          3. Click expand icon to expand reporting view
          4. Click download icon to export to Excel

          View Worker Timesheets:

          1. Click Workers tab → select desired worker
          2. Scroll to Time & Expense section to view current week timesheet
          3. To view historical timesheets: select desired week ending from drop-down
          ✎My Notes
          Lyft

          Conditional Offer Templates

          Editable offer letter templates. Click any template to edit. Use the + button to add a new template for a different role.

          ✎My Notes
          Bullhorn Placement Setup

          Account Setup — Key Fields

          All critical Bullhorn fields. Yellow-labelled fields are editable — click to fill in. Saves automatically per client profile.

          Click any field below to type. Data saves automatically to the active client. Switching clients loads that client’s data.
          1
          Contract / Payroll Placement
          Core placement fields
          ▼
          FieldValueStatus
          Employment TypeCritical
          StatusSubmittedStandard
          Assignee TypeStandard
          Start DateStandard
          Contract Start DateStandard
          Scheduled EndStandard
          Business UnitCritical
          Worker Comp CodeCritical
          Timesheet ApproverCritical
          Account ManagerStandard
          RecruiterStandard
          Backup Timesheet ApproverOptional
          IC FlagStandard
          Falloff InitiatorOptional
          Falloff ReasonOptional
          2
          Work Location
          Client site address
          ▼
          FieldValueStatus
          Work LocationClientStandard
          CountryCritical
          State / ProvinceCritical
          StreetCritical
          Unit / Apt / SuiteOptional
          CityCritical
          CountyCritical
          Zip / Postal CodeCritical
          Work Location NotesOptional
          3
          Pay Rates
          Bill and pay rate setup
          ▼
          FieldValueStatus
          Paying CompanyRaise Group Public Benefit CorporationStandard
          Pay/Bill Effective DateStandard
          Bill CurrencyCritical
          Pay CurrencyCritical
          Bill UnitCritical
          Pay UnitCritical
          Bill RateStandard
          Pay RateStandard
          OT Bill RateCritical
          OT Pay RateCritical
          DT Bill RateStandard
          DT Pay RateStandard
          Bill Stat at 1.5Optional
          Pay Stat at 1.5Optional
          4
          Payroll Information
          Hours, pay frequency, OT rules
          ▼
          FieldValueStatus
          Time and Expense SystemStandard
          Timesheet FormatStandard
          Hours Per DayCritical
          Part Time (<20 hrs/wk)Standard
          CE Pay FrequencyCritical
          Overtime RuleCritical
          Overtime Rule (Contract)Standard
          Payroll CommentsStandard
          VMS Alias IDOptional
          Create Expense Placement in 1SBOStandard
          Enrolled in ConciergeOptional
          5
          Agreement Information
          Branch, legal terms, non-solicitation
          ▼
          FieldValueStatus
          Placement is SupervisorStandard
          Branch for AgreementCritical
          Job DescriptionOptional
          Agreement OtherOptional
          Agreement ConditionsOptional
          First Day DetailsOptional
          Vacation PayCritical
          Notice PeriodCritical
          Non-Solicitation PeriodCritical
          Non-Competition PeriodCritical
          Package TagOptional
          Pre-submission checklist
          ✅Before Submitting — Verify All Critical Fields
          • Employment Type filled in
          • Business Unit filled in
          • Worker Comp Code filled in
          • Timesheet Approver filled in
          • Country, State, Street, City, County, Zip all filled in
          • Bill & Pay Currency filled in
          • Bill Unit & Pay Unit filled in
          • OT Bill Rate & OT Pay Rate filled in
          • Hours Per Day filled in
          • CE Pay Frequency filled in
          • Overtime Rule filled in
          • Branch for Agreement filled in
          • Vacation Pay, Notice Period, Non-Solicitation, Non-Competition filled in
          BASF — Canada & US

          Onboarding Workflow

          Step-by-step process for BASF Canada and US candidates. Trained by Hemlata Thorat — April 14, 2026.

          ✎ Edit Page
          ✓ Saved
          🇨🇦
          Regions
          Canada + US
          👥
          Types
          Students & Non-Students
          📅
          Billing
          Bi-Weekly
          📱
          CS Rep
          Hemlata Thorat
          High volume account — 100+ active onboardings at any time. Speed and accuracy are both critical.
          Phase 1 — Open Onboarding Request & Check Location
          Salesforce → BASF Onboarding Tracker → Candidate Profile
          ~0:15
          1a
          Salesforce BASF Onboarding Tracker
          Service Console → Onboarding Trackers → Raise BASF Onboarding Tracker
          ▼

          Open the tracker to see all active candidates sorted by onboarding status (50+ items at any time).

          Raise BASF Onboarding Tracker — 50+ items sorted by Onboarding Status
          1b
          Open candidate profile — verify location
          Click candidate name → profile popup shows city, province, status
          ▼

          Click the candidate to see the popup with location. This determines the Assignee Type.

          Candidate popup — Francine Tshilumba, Location: Lethbridge Alberta, Email, Mobile, GP ID: 4671735, Status: Hired-Contract
          1c
          Placement Details tab
          GP ID, Record Type, Job, CS Rep, Onboarding Rep
          ▼
          Placement Details — GP ID: 92953, Record Type: Contract/Payroll, Job: 169903, CS Rep: Hemlata Thorat, Onboarding Rep: Hemlata Thorat, Division: R-ONE BASF
          1d
          Confirm candidate address — city & province
          Candidate profile → address fields
          ▼
          Candidate address — City: Lethbridge, State/Province: Alberta, Country: Canada → use Assignee Type ABCE
          ⚠ Key decision
          • Alberta, Canada → Assignee Type = ABCE
          1e
          Placement full view & pipeline stages
          Submitted → Approved → Completed pipeline
          ▼
          Placement — Francine Tshilumba, BASF Corporation, Hiring Assistant (PRE-ID), Status: Submitted
          1f
          Set Assignee Type & verify Business Unit
          Contract/Payroll Placement section
          ▼
          Contract/Payroll — Employment Type: Payroll, Assignee Type: AB-CE, Business Unit: 2843-BASF(PRS CAN), Status: Submitted
          Canada BASF placement fields
          Employment TypePayroll
          Assignee TypeAB-CE (Alberta)
          Business Unit2843 - BASF (PRS CAN)
          Account ManagerMarci Roy
          Phase 2 — Send Onboarding Documents (HelloSign)
          Select Package → Remove Retiree Doc → Send Signature Request
          ~3:17
          2a
          Select package & placement
          Search "basf" → choose package → select placement
          ▼
          Package options: BASF-All (1969.66), BASF-CAN Only (138.47), BASF-CAN Students Only (0), BASF-US Only (404.79)
          Which package to use
          • BASF - All → most cases
          • BASF - CAN Only → Canada non-student
          • BASF - CAN Students Only → Canada students
          • BASF - US Only → US candidates
          BASF - All selected
          Placement dropdown: 152254, BASF Corporation, Hiring Assistant (PRE-ID), Submitted
          2b
          Remove Retiree document — EVERY TIME
          Onboarding Request Items (7) → delete Retiree doc
          ▼
          7 items listed — dropdown showing Delete on Agreement & Waiver (Non Retiree)
          ⚠ Do this every single time
          • Click dropdown → Delete the Retiree document before sending
          2c
          Review request & send
          Full request view → Awaiting Signatures → send
          ▼
          Request-88106 — Package: BASF-All, Signature Status: Awaiting Signatures, 7 items in right panel
          Onboarding Requests (2): Request-88107 and Request-88106
          Smaller set: BASF-CAN Safety Acknowledgement + Access Badge Form
          Phase 3 — Order Background Check
          Triton (Canada)  |  First Advantage / Sterling (US)
          ~4:59
          3a
          Triton — fill candidate info & select package
          Online Forms → Single Form → Individual Services - Social Media
          ▼
          Triton Online Forms — First Name, Last Name, Email (required), Language: English/French
          Package: Individual Services - Social Media selected. Method: Paperless
          3b
          Select BGC services by candidate type
          Students: 2 items  |  Non-Students: 4 items
          ▼
          Services list: Criminal Record Check, Employment Verification (qty), Education Verification (qty), Public Safety, and others
          🎓 Students (2)
          ✓ Criminal Record Check
          ✓ Public Safety
          💼 Non-Students (4)
          ✓ Criminal Record Check
          ✓ Employment VerificationQty: 5
          ✓ Education VerificationQty: 3
          ✓ Public Safety
          Employment Verification qty dropdown with 5 selected (blue)
          3c
          First Advantage — US BGC order
          Start Order → Invite Candidate → Consent Plus → select position
          ▼
          First Advantage nav: Start Order → Invite Candidate | Manual Order
          Position options: A-LA-Carte, Drug Test Only, Standard Package, Standard Package Without EMP, TC22 Laboratory Technician
          Completed order — BASF CONTRACTOR RAISE GROUP, Standard Package, Add Ons: Education Verification + Drug & Health Test, Order Result: Review
          Phase 4 — Upload to Agile One (AVM)
          AgileOne → Assignment Screening → BGC Certificate
          ~15:25
          4a
          AgileOne homepage — To Do List
          Check Assignment Screenings Completion Requested
          ▼
          To Do List — Assignment Ending 120 Days (3), Assignment Screenings Completion Requested (3)
          Documents: 2026 BASF BGC Certificate, AgileOne BASF Adjudication Process, Canada Safety Acknowledgement, Supplier FAQs
          BASF policy: Prior positive drug test = no future reconsideration.
          4b
          Assignment Screening grid
          5 required docs for Canada — all must reach Completed status
          ▼
          5 items: Agreement and Waiver CW-CAN, Background Verification Cert-CAN, ILSS-CAN, NDA-CAN, Safety Acknowledgement-CAN. All Submitted ✓, Completed ☐, Status: Screening Pending, Required ✓
          4c
          BASF Canada Files — BGC certificate templates
          BASF Canada Inc. → Files tab
          ▼
          BASF Canada Inc. Files: Adjudication Guidance, 2026-BGC-Certificate (100KB), Example BGC Certificate (302KB), AgileOne BASF CW Adjudication Process
          Billing Contact = always ONTARIO regardless of province (Srila Mundlur note).
          4d
          Fill BGC Certificate form
          Page 1: worker details  |  Page 2: BGC screenshot (mandatory)
          ▼
          BGC Certificate CONFIDENTIAL — CW Legal Name (First/Middle/Last), CW City, CW State/Province
          ⚠ Page 2 is mandatory
          • Screenshot the completed BGC results from Triton/First Advantage → attach on Page 2
          • If BASF sent adjudication approval email → screenshot it and attach too
          • Agile One will reject the form without this
          US Placement Files: Audit Trail, Confidentiality Nonsolicitation Agreement, Policy Guidelines W2, Raise Acknowledgement and Waiver Form
          Phase 5 — Post-Contract: Timesheet Instructions & Salesforce
          Send timesheet email → Mark Approved → Fill required fields
          After signing
          5a
          Send timesheet instructions immediately after signing
          Case → timesheet email with AgileOne credentials
          ▼
          Timesheet email details
          SystemAgileOne
          DeadlineNoon Monday for the prior week
          RecommendedSubmit Friday → manager approves Monday morning
          PaymentWeekly — deposit each Friday
          Send IMMEDIATELY after contract is signed.
          5b
          Mark Approved in Salesforce & fill fields
          BASF Tracker → Mark Approved → Start Date, Work Location, Comp Code, Job Description
          ▼
          Step 1
          Mark as Approved
          Go to Salesforce placement → Mark Status as Complete / Approved.
          Step 2
          Fill required fields
          Must fill: Start Date • Work Location • Comp Code • Job Description
          Do NOT fill: Bill Rate or other rate fields.
          Step 3
          Update tracker notes
          Add notes e.g. "HT-04/14 - Contract signed, timesheet sent, SF approved".
          Quick Reference
          🇨🇦Canada Summary
          Assignee type (AB)ABCE
          Business Unit2843 - BASF (PRS CAN)
          Welcome emailCE Welcome Email
          BGC systemTriton
          Student BGCCriminal + Public Safety (2)
          Non-student BGCCriminal + Employment (5yr) + Education (3yr) + Public Safety (4)
          Billing ContactAlways ONTARIO
          🇺🇸US Summary
          Welcome emailW2 Onboarding Welcome Email
          BGC systemFirst Advantage (Sterling)
          WorkflowConsent Plus
          PackageDepends on position type
          Drug testBASF notifies per candidate
          Backup contactSrila (5 yrs exp)
          ✎My Notes
          BASF — Canada & US

          Candidate Onboarding — Step-by-Step Guide

          For Coordinators. Covers all steps from receiving the request to uploading final documents in Agile One. Source: BASF Onboarding Training Guide.

          ✎ Edit Page
          ✓ Saved
          💻Systems Used
          Candidate SystemRC App / Candidate Profiles
          Canada BGCTriton
          US BGCFirst Advantage (Sterling)
          Document UploadAgile One
          ⚠Top Mistakes to Avoid
          ✗ Retiree docAlways delete from BSF All before sending
          ✗ Wrong emailCE for Canada • W2 for US
          ✗ BGC Page 2Screenshot is mandatory — never skip
          ✗ NDA addressMust be complete or resend required
          📥 Section 1 — Receiving the Onboarding Request
          Steps 1–5
          1–5
          Open request → candidate profile → verify location
          ▼
          1
          Receive the onboarding request
          Click the link provided in the request to open it.
          Step 1 — Onboarding request received. Click the link to open.
          2
          Open the candidate profile from the request
          3
          Navigate to the Placement tab
          Steps 2–3 — Candidate profile and Placement tab
          4
          Verify the location
          Check the city and state on the job posting.
          5
          Double-check candidate profile
          Go back to the candidate profile and confirm city and state match the job.
          Steps 4–5 — Verify city and state match between job and candidate profile
          💻 Section 2 — Setting Up the Candidate Portal (RC App)
          Steps 6–8
          6–8
          Select Assignee Type → start RC App portal
          Must be done before sending documents
          ▼
          6
          In Placement Details, scroll down and select the Assignee Type
          Example: If location is Alberta → select ABCE as the Assignee Type.
          Step 6 — Scroll to Placement Details and select Assignee Type (e.g. ABCE for Alberta)
          7
          Go to the candidate profile and locate the RC App icon
          8
          Click the RC App icon
          A username, status, profile type, and onboarding flow will be created automatically. The candidate will receive a notification to begin their onboarding.
          Steps 7–8 — RC App icon in candidate profile. Click to create portal and send notification.
          ⚠ Starting the portal is mandatory. Without this step, the candidate will NOT receive the onboarding notification.
          ✉ Section 3 — Sending the Welcome Email
          Steps 9–13
          9–13
          Create Case → select correct welcome email → send
          ▼
          9
          Click More → Create a Case
          Do this after the RC App is created.
          10
          Select the Welcome Email template
          Step 10 — Select the correct welcome email template
          🇨🇦 CANADA CANDIDATE
          Select: “CE Welcome Email”
          🇺🇸 US CANDIDATE
          Select: “W2 Onboarding Welcome Email”
          ⚠ Always select the correct welcome email. Sending the wrong template to the candidate will cause delays.
          11
          Add a dot (.) in the required field
          Without it, the email will not save.
          12
          Fill in CS Support, then click Next
          13
          Click Send to send the welcome email to the candidate
          Steps 11–13 — Add dot, fill CS Support, click Next, then Send
          📋 Section 4 — Sending Onboarding Documents (BSF Forms)
          Steps 14–17
          14–17
          Select BSF All + BSF Canada → remove Retiree doc → send signature requests
          ▼
          14
          Navigate to candidate profile → Onboarding → select the Placement
          15
          Select the document package
          Both Students and Non-Students: select BSF All and BSF Canada. These contain the required assignment documents for both types.
          Step 15 — Select BSF All and BSF Canada packages
          ⚠ BEFORE SENDING: Navigate to "BSF All" and review the document list. Find "BSF Agreement and Waiver and Retiree" — click on it and DELETE it. All current candidates are non-retirees. Do this every single time.
          16
          After removing the Retiree document, go to the Request and click Send Signature Request
          Step 16 — Send Signature Request button
          17
          A pop-up will appear — click to confirm and send
          ⚠ There are TWO documents to send signature requests for. Repeat the Send Signature Request step for both.
          🔍 Section 5 — Ordering Background Check (BGC) Items
          Steps 18–29
          🇨🇦
          Canada → Use Triton
          🇺🇸
          US → Use First Advantage (Sterling)
          5A 🇨🇦
          Canada BGC — Triton
          Steps 18–25
          ▼
          18
          Log into Triton
          19
          Go to Online Forms for Canada
          Step 19 — Triton Online Forms for Canada
          20
          Enter the candidate's first name, last name, and email address
          21
          Click Individual Services → Social Media to expand the services list
          22
          Select the appropriate services based on candidate type
          🎓 STUDENT CANDIDATES — 2 items
          ✓ Criminal Record Check
          ✓ Public Safety
          💼 NON-STUDENT CANDIDATES — 4 items
          ✓ Criminal Record Check
          ✓ Employment Verification — 5 years / 5 employers
          ✓ Education Verification — 3 years
          ✓ Public Safety
          Step 22 — Triton services list (part 1) — select appropriate items
          Step 22 — Triton services list (part 2) — Employment & Education Verification with quantities
          23
          For "Position Applying For" field
          Copy the job title directly from the Placement tab and paste it here.
          Step 23 — Copy job title from Placement tab and paste into "Position Applying For" field in Triton
          24
          Click Submit
          25
          Verify submission
          Go to Reports in Triton to confirm the BGC was submitted successfully. Status should show: "Email Sent to Candidate."
          Step 25 — Triton Reports showing "Email Sent to Candidate" status
          ⚠ Canada drug/medical screening is still being confirmed by BASF. Wait for confirmation from BASF before ordering any drug test for Canada candidates. For US candidates, BASF will notify you if a drug or physical test is required.
          5B 🇺🇸
          US BGC — First Advantage (Sterling)
          Steps 26–29
          ▼
          26
          Log into First Advantage (Sterling)
          27
          Click Start Order → Invite Candidate
          Step 27 — First Advantage: Start Order → Invite Candidate
          28
          Fill in the candidate details and select the required services
          If a drug test is required: select it from the services list. For all other services: select the Standard Package.
          29
          Click Next, then Submit
          ⚠ BGC services for US candidates vary by position. BASF will notify you of any specific requirements for each candidate.
          📊 Section 6 — Monitoring BGC Status
          Steps 30–32
          30–32
          View Request → check status → wait for Outlook notification
          ▼
          30
          In Triton or First Advantage, go to View Request to check status per candidate
          31
          Status meanings
          StatusMeaning
          Email Sent to CandidateCandidate has not yet acted on the BGC request
          ClearedBGC item has passed ✓
          PendingBGC item is still in progress
          Step 31 — BGC status view in Triton/First Advantage showing Cleared, Pending statuses
          32
          You will receive an email notification in Outlook when BGC items are cleared
          ⚠ BGC notification emails only arrive when items are cleared. Monitor Triton/First Advantage for pending statuses — do not wait for emails to check progress.
          📁 Section 7 — Uploading Documents in Agile One
          Steps 33–37
          33–37
          Log in → find candidate → review & upload all documents
          Only after candidate has signed all forms and BGC is cleared
          ▼
          33
          Log into Agile One
          34
          On the homepage, locate the candidate
          They will appear once they have been engaged in the system.
          Step 34 — Agile One homepage showing active candidates
          35
          Click on the candidate's name to open their profile
          36
          Go to Assignment Screening
          The signed documents (BSF All, BSF Canada) will appear here once the candidate has signed them.
          Step 36 — Assignment Screening showing signed documents ready to upload
          Signed documents from BSF All and BSF Canada packages will automatically reflect in the candidate's files under the Placement section once signed.
          37
          Review each document carefully before uploading
          DocumentWhat to Check
          Agreement & WaiverCandidate signature only required — no fields to fill in
          NDA ⚠Verify candidate has entered their complete address. If incomplete, resend the form.
          BGC Form ⚠Fill in: candidate name, date of birth, city, state, and how the BGC was processed. See Section 8 for the screenshot attachment requirement.
          ⚠ Expect frequent back-and-forth with Agile One. They require complete and accurate information on all forms. If anything is missing or incorrect, Agile One will ask you to resend the form. Be thorough to avoid delays.
          📸 Section 8 — Completing the BGC Form (Screenshot Requirement)
          Steps 38–44
          38–44
          BGC form Page 2 — attach screenshot of cleared BGC (MANDATORY)
          ▼
          The BGC form has two pages. Page 2 requires a screenshot showing the BGC is completed. This is MANDATORY — do not skip it.
          38
          Open Triton (or First Advantage for US) in a new browser tab
          39
          Go to the candidate's BGC submission and navigate to Overview
          Step 39 — Agile One / BGC system Overview screen
          40
          Take a screenshot of the entire Overview screen
          The screenshot must show all BGC items as Cleared.
          41
          Go to Page 2 of the BGC form in Agile One
          42
          Attach the screenshot to Page 2 of the BGC form
          Steps 42–44 — Attaching the BGC cleared screenshot to Page 2 of the BGC form in Agile One
          ⚠ If BASF is adjudicating a BGC (reviewing and approving a candidate despite a flag) and they send you an approval email, you must also take a screenshot of that approval email and attach it to Page 2 of the BGC form, in addition to the Triton/Sterling screenshot.
          📈 Section 9 — Checking the BASF Onboarding Tracker
          Steps 45–47
          45–47
          Monitor the tracker — 100+ active candidates at any time
          ▼
          45
          Access the tracker from the shared folder (BSF Onboarding Tracker)
          46
          The tracker shows all candidates currently in the onboarding process
          47
          When a candidate fully starts (completes onboarding), they will be removed from this tracker
          Steps 45–47 — BASF Onboarding Tracker showing all active candidates
          ⚠ The onboarding tracker may show 100+ active candidates at any time. Prioritize candidates based on start date urgency. Candidates cannot receive their BASF IDs from Agile One until onboarding is fully complete — process as quickly and accurately as possible.
          ⚡ Section 10 — Quick Reference Summary
          #ActionWhere
          1Receive onboarding request & click linkEmail / Notification
          2Open candidate profile → Placement tab → verify locationCandidate System
          3Select Assignee Type in Placement DetailsCandidate System
          4Click RC App icon to create portal & send notificationCandidate System
          5Create Case → Send Welcome Email (CE for Canada / W2 for US)Candidate System
          6Onboarding → Select BSF All + BSF Canada → Remove Retiree doc → Send Signature Requests (x2)Candidate System
          7Order BGC items (Triton for Canada / Sterling for US)Triton / First Advantage
          8Monitor BGC status in Reports / View RequestTriton / First Advantage
          9Once forms signed → upload documents in Agile OneAgile One
          10Complete BGC form: fill details + attach screenshot (Page 2)Agile One
          11Verify accuracy of NDA (complete address required)Agile One
          12Update BASF Onboarding TrackerShared Tracker
          💡 Tips & Common Mistakes to Avoid
          • Always double-check city/state on the job matches the candidate profile before proceeding.
          • Never skip the Retiree document removal step — always delete from BSF All before sending.
          • Send the correct welcome email — CE for Canada, W2 for US.
          • Non-student BGCs: Employment Verification = 5 years, Education Verification = 3 years.
          • The BGC screenshot on Page 2 is mandatory — do not skip it.
          • NDA must have the candidate's complete address. Incomplete = resend required.
          • If BASF sends an adjudication approval email, screenshot it and attach it to the BGC form.
          • If a candidate is stuck, refer them to the 'How to Complete Onboarding' guide.
          ✎My Notes
          All Clients

          Onboarding Packages

          Per-client onboarding package details — documents to send, Salesforce package name, conditional offer letter, and notes. All fields are editable and save automatically.

          Click any field to edit. All data saves automatically per client profile. Switch between clients using the tabs below.
          B
          BASF
          Canada & US — CS Rep: Hemlata Thorat
          Salesforce Package Details
          BASF - All  |  BASF - CAN Only  |  BASF - CAN Students Only  |  BASF - US Only
          ABCE (Alberta, Canada)
          2843 - BASF (PRS CAN)  |  2842 (Canada)  |  7528 (US)
          Bi-Weekly
          Documents to Send
          Check off documents as you send them. Edit the list to add or remove items.
          🇨🇦 Canada Candidates
          BSF Hall (Onboarding)
          Both students and non-students
          BSF Canada
          Both students and non-students
          BASF - Agreement & Waiver (Non Retiree)
          REMOVE before sending — always
          NDA CW Invention and Secrecy Agreement
          Verify complete address before uploading to Agile One
          CAN Safety Acknowledgement
          Required upload for all CW in Canada
          Background Verification Certificate (BGC Form)
          Fill + attach BGC screenshot on Page 2 (mandatory)
          + Add document
          🇺🇸 US Candidates
          Confidentiality Nonsolicitation Agreement All States
          All US candidates
          Consent to Provide Personal Information (US)
          All US candidates
          Policy Guidelines W2 (US)
          All US candidates
          Raise - Acknowledgement and Waiver Form (CE/W2)
          All US candidates
          + Add document
          Background Check Packages
          🇨🇦 Canada BGC (Triton)
          Students (2 items):
          ✓ Criminal Record Check ✓ Public Safety Verification
          Non-Students (4 items):
          ✓ Criminal Record Check ✓ Employment Verification (5 yrs) ✓ Education Verification (3 yrs) ✓ Public Safety Verification
          🇺🇸 US BGC (First Advantage / Sterling)
          Standard Package (most positions):
          Standard Package (select from First Advantage). Drug test if required by BASF for the specific position. BASF will notify for each candidate.
          Position packages available:
          A-LA-Carte Drug Test Only Standard Package Standard Package Without EMP TC22 Laboratory Technician
          Conditional Offer Letter
          CE Welcome Email (Canada)  |  W2 Onboarding Welcome Email (US)
          Raise Console → Create Case
          Contingent on BGC clearance. Drug test pending BASF confirmation (Canada). BASF notifies per candidate (US).
          After RC App is created. Before or alongside BGC order.
          Add a dot (.) in the required field or the case will not save. Select CS Support before clicking Next. Ensure correct template: CE for Canada, W2 for US.
          Post-Signing / Post-Contract Steps
          1. Send timesheet instructions to candidate immediately after contract is signed (AgileOne credentials + deadline info) 2. Mark placement as Approved in Salesforce 3. Fill required Salesforce fields: Start Date, Work Location, Comp Code, Job Description 4. Do NOT fill Bill Rate or other rate fields
          Package Notes
          Billing Contact = always ONTARIO regardless of province (AgileOne pays ON taxes). Retiree document must be removed every single time from BSF All. High volume account — 100+ active onboardings at any time.
          A
          Applied Materials
          Austin, TX — EOR — CS Rep: Pamela Randall (EWOps)
          Salesforce Package Details
          Fieldglass Resource Order (no Salesforce package — all managed through Fieldglass)
          TX-W2 (Texas EOR)
          7565 - Exxon (PRS) — verify per placement
          Weekly (via Fieldglass, consolidated Wednesdays)
          Documents to Send
          All AMAT EOR Workers
          Contingent Worker Confidentiality Agreement
          Required at resource order stage. Upload to Fieldglass.
          Exhibit A — U.S. Export Compliance Certification
          Mandatory for all non-employees. Return with Agreement.
          Raise Employment Agreement + Tax Docs (I-9, W4)
          Raise EOR employment onboarding documents
          COI Disclosure Form
          Reviewed by AMAT Security. Upload to Fieldglass.
          Worker Photo (for badging)
          Due Monday before training week. White background only.
          + Add document
          Background Check Package
          BGC completed during pipeline build (before resource order). AMAT Security marks complete in Fieldglass. Criminal, Drug, Employment required. E-Verify required. 7-year lookback, disposition date only. See BG Matrix page for full adjudication criteria.
          Conditional Offer Letter
          Raise EOR Employment Agreement (W2, Texas)
          Raise EOR onboarding system
          BGC clearance by AMAT Security. COI review. System activation by AMAT EW Ops. All Fieldglass activities completed.
          At Stage 8 (Resource Order). Must be uploaded to Fieldglass before resource order activation.
          Exhibit B (State Invention Assignment) required if worker is in CA, KS, IL, MN, or WA. Applied Travel Rules (Exhibit B) NOT required for EOR workers. 18-month max assignment period.
          Package Notes
          All documents uploaded to Fieldglass. Payment via Fieldglass within 45 days. 0.5% Fieldglass VMS fee added to bill rate. Activation deadline: Thursday before training week (Friday 10AM CST absolute latest).
          Use this template to add onboarding package details for a new client. Fill in all fields and they will save automatically.
          New Client Details
          Documents to Send
          Document List
          + Add document
          Conditional Offer Letter
          BGC Package
          Package Notes
          Applied Materials

          Shifts & OT Guide

          How to calculate shift-adjusted pay rates and overtime for Manufacturing and Logistics roles. Texas follows FLSA.

          3-Step Process:  1. Identify shift type (Manufacturing or Logistics) 2. Apply shift differential → get shift rate 3. If over 40 hrs/week → apply OT at 1.5×

          Shift Rate Formula

          Shift Rate = Base Pay × (1 + Differential%)

          Example: $20.00 × 1.10 = $22.00

          OT Rate Formula (FLSA – TX)

          OT Rate = Shift Rate × 1.5

          OT kicks in after 40 hrs/week only. No daily OT, no Sunday OT, no DT unless holiday set in Fieldglass.

          🏭Manufacturing Shifts
          ShiftDifferentialFormulaExample on $20/hrOT Rate (×1.5)
          Day / 1st Shift0%$20.00 × 1.00$20.00$30.00
          Swing / 2nd Shift10%$20.00 × 1.10$22.00$33.00
          Night / 3rd Shift13%$20.00 × 1.13$22.60$33.90
          Weekend Day (10CWW)10%$20.00 × 1.10$22.00$33.00
          Weekend Swing (10CWW)18%$20.00 × 1.18$23.60$35.40
          Weekend Night (10CWW)23%$20.00 × 1.23$24.60$36.90
          📦Logistics Shifts
          ShiftDifferentialFormulaExample on $20/hrOT Rate (×1.5)
          1A / 1B (Day, 10CWW)5%$20.00 × 1.05$21.00$31.50
          2A / 2B (Night, 10CWW)15%$20.00 × 1.15$23.00$34.50
          Day Swing5%$20.00 × 1.05$21.00$31.50
          Grave Swing12.5%$20.00 × 1.125$22.50$33.75
          💻Fieldglass OT & DT Codes
          CodeDescriptionCodeDescription
          US_OT1Standard OvertimeUS_DTDouble Time
          US_OTSWINGOT – Swing ShiftUS_DTSWINGDT – Swing
          US_OTNIGHTOT – Night ShiftUS_DTNIGHTDT – Night
          US_OTWKNDOT – WeekendUS_DTWKNDDT – Weekend
          US_OTWKND_SWINGOT – Weekend SwingUS_DTWKND_SWINGDT – Weekend Swing
          US_OTWKND_NIGHTOT – Weekend NightUS_DTWKND_NIGHTDT – Weekend Night
          US_OTHOLOT – HolidayUS_DTHOLDT – Holiday
          US_OTHOL_SWINGOT – Holiday SwingUS_DTHOL_SWINGDT – Holiday Swing
          US_OTHOL_NIGHTOT – Holiday NightUS_DTHOL_NIGHTDT – Holiday Night
          ✎My Notes
          Applied Materials

          Client Intake

          Full intake document — entity, contacts, IDs, billing, compliance, PPE, and service scope.

          🏢Entity & System IDs
          FieldValue
          Legal EntityApplied Materials, Inc.
          IMG Legal Entity (MSA)US: Raise Group Public Benefit Corporation
          FEIN94-1655526
          Phone408-727-5555
          HQ Address3050 Bowers Avenue, Santa Clara, CA 95054
          Websitehttps://www.appliedmaterials.com/
          SF Sales Client ID715616
          SF Sales Client Contact ID3566138
          SF zBilling Client ID779761
          SF zBilling Contact IDTexas: 3714566 | California: 3714574
          SF zBilling Timesheet Approver ID3714575
          BU7452 – Applied Materials (MDS) | 7593 – Applied Materials (PRS)
          👤Key Contacts
          RoleContact
          Account ManagerRick Roberts / Tim Rhodes (until AM is identified)
          Primary Client ContactPamela Randall — Category Business Manager, Global Staffing, Global Sourcing & Procurement
          pamela_randall@amat.com | PHDIndirect_NASuppliers@amat.com
          Client AP (Invoicing)EW_Ops@amat.com
          EWOps Leader (Escalation)Srisalini Myneni
          H&S Contact — TXJason Allgood, Manager EHS — Jason_Allgood@amat.com
          H&S Contact — MAEd Akerley, Manager EHS — Edward_Akerley@amat.com
          🎯Services & Scope
          Services: MDS (Curation / Talent Community / EOR / AOR) + lift and shift for pre-ID workers (transition). On-site model — connect with workers and hiring managers, anticipate upcoming opens, compliance risk management.
          Go-live: MDS first week of March · Lift & shift mid-April (small groups, final 50–60) · Singapore end of May
          States: Texas – Austin (primary) | Future: California – Santa Clara, Singapore
          VMS fee: 0.5% deducted at invoicing (time & material SOWs)
          Previous EOR: Magnit. Pre-ID workers: where local law requires notice/severance, Applied will provide or reimburse as invoiced.
          💰Billing & Payment
          FieldValue
          Invoicing frequencyWeekly (US) | Monthly (Singapore — supplier pulls FG report, submits in Taulia)
          Payment termsN45 (markup assumes weekly billing; lower markup available for N30)
          VMSFieldglass — auto-generates invoices every Wednesday after time approval
          POAutomated per resource order in FG (US). Singapore: blanket PO outside FG.
          CurrencyUSD (US) | SGD (Singapore)
          Who paysApplied Materials directly
          Work weekUS: Monday–Sunday | Singapore: Calendar Monthly
          CE/W2 pay frequencyBi-weekly or weekly
          🔍Compliance, BGC & Legal
          BGC provider: First Advantage — AMAT pays. 7-year lookback. Web-based portal. Required before badge issued. Drug screen required in US only.
          BGC disqualifiers (7-year lookback): Any felony · violence/assault/stalking/DV · sex offense · theft · weapons · death/injury conviction (fine >$500 or incarceration >30 days) · 2+ DUI/DWI · 2+ narcotics possession. Marijuana misdemeanor eligible if 2+ yrs old.
          Tenure: US = 18 months max, 90-day break. Singapore = 24 months, 90-day break. Non-compete: CE=N/A, IC=90 days. Non-solicitation: CE=90d, IC=180d. Notice: CE=2 weeks, IC=10 days.
          Required docs: Contingent Worker Confidentiality Agreement (Attachment I, MSA p.22). No equipment agreement for bulk LI hires. No Travel Rules needed for LI.
          Export regulation: Heightened screening for citizens/PRs of: China, Russia, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Belarus, Burma, Cambodia, Cuba, Georgia, Iran, Iraq, Kazakhstan, North Korea, Kyrgyzstan, Laos, Libya, Macau, Moldova, Mongolia, Sudan, Syria, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Ukraine, Uzbekistan, Vietnam. In some cases, commercially infeasible to obtain license.
          Records retention: 3 years. Re-hire rule: Agents dismissed for cause from prior AMAT assignment must not return. Business Conduct: appliedmaterials.com/content/dam/site/company/about/doc/corporate-governance/sbc.pdf
          🦺PPE & Safety Training
          Workers must bring own PPE in good condition: safety glasses, steel-toed boots. Only loaned items: cleanroom attire (jumpsuit, hat, gloves, shoe covers, goggles).
          Mandatory certifications (where role requires): Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) · Electrical · Radiation · Crane/hoist/lifting · Hazmat handling · Fall protection · Hearing protection · Powered equipment · Ladder safety · PPE · Respiratory protection · Spill prevention · Hazardous waste.
          Training required: All environmental, safety, H&S, information security, IP protection, ethics, and specialized training required by AMAT must be completed before work begins.
          Badge: Required for network access or unescorted site access. Must complete "Contractor/Supplier Access Form" and pass BGC before issuance. Worn at/above waist at all times.
          ✎My Notes
          Applied Materials

          Fieldglass Submission Process

          Training notes from 04/17/2026 Fieldglass session — key clarifications, submission steps, hashtags, activity items, and reporting.

          📌Key Clarifications — 04/17/2026 Training
          ⚠

          The background check could be any company — not exclusive to one provider.

          ⚠

          Assessment results are advised to be uploaded under Files instead of Credentials.

          ⚠

          The photo is not uploaded to Fieldglass until the worker has been assigned.

          ℹ

          Candidates will be able to access Fieldglass to add their own timesheets.

          🔤Fieldglass Key Terms
          TermWhat It Means
          APM Always for my numbers — starts with APM. This is the main identifier prefix in Fieldglass.
          JP / Job Request This is the job number.
          Resource Order Where we will attach candidates to the job.
          Hash Tagging When we will know we can submit.
          JP Number Where we will attach the candidate to.
          📋Submission Steps — Pre-Qualified (Candidate Submitted)
          1

          Once submitted, add a #Submitted and #OPnumber hashtag.

          2

          The system will ask you to enter a security ID.

          3

          Enter the Final Bill Rate.

          4

          If the candidate is rejected, we cannot delete them — tag as #Withdrawn.

          5

          If there are any YES answers in the COI, must add #COI in the Workforce Module.

          6

          If there is a rehire, make sure to add #Rehire.

          7

          Once you accept a resource order, we can accept the Workforce Module.

          ⚙️Activity Items in Fieldglass
          a. Attach the COI — extended agreement needs to be attached. ↳ If there's an error, a new activity will be assigned by the supplier.
          b. Flag that you have launched the background (just flag it). ↳ BWA candidate name must match profile. ↳ The worker has access to change their own shift. ↳ Payroll deadline (Raise) every Monday. ↳ If timesheets are approved/denied, we will be getting an email.
          c. They should not be registered until they start on their first day. ↳ Fieldglass won't be provided until onboarding is done with AMAT.
          d. Add #Started hashtag.
          🏷️Applied "Other Tags"
          #APMJP00 (Request to tag resource)
          #Rehire (Rehire Pending)
          #Hired (Resource order created)
          #Activated (XID Generated)
          🏷️Supplier Tags
          #COI (There is a conflict of interest)
          #Submitted, #APMUP (Workforce tagged)
          #Accepted (Resource Order Accepted)
          #Activity (All activity items are completed)
          #Started (Resource began assignment)
          📊Reporting & Invoicing Tips
          → Talia is automated.
          → Workforce Report should show people we are onboarding.
          → Set the time always to tomorrow (Consolidated Report) for the Resource Module.

          Consolidated Invoice ID

          ↳ Very important. We can use this in Talia to show if an invoice has been paid.
          ↳ Invoice line item = Timesheet number.
          ↳ We can run this every week.
          ✎My Notes
          Click below to add notes
          Applied Materials — Salesforce Workflow

          Candidate Onboarding Workflow

          Two-phase workflow: Phase 1 covers pre-requisition vetting through talent pool. Phase 2 covers active job placement through to Hired.

          ✎ Edit Page
          ✓ Saved
          Phase 1 step
          Phase 2 step
          Pre-handoff / offer step
          Automated trigger
          Key milestone
          AMAT Tracker update

          Phase 1 — Pre-Requisition Candidate Vetting

          All items completed before handoff to talent pool

          Pre-handoff screening
          All items completed before handoff

          Job Application form reviewed

          Verified and on file

          Phone interview conducted

          Interview completed with candidate

          Recruiter screening form completed

          Form submitted and reviewed

          Rehire form completed

          Returning candidates only

          if applicable
          Rehire cleared

          🤝 HANDOFF — Resume arrives in SF

          We receive a case — this is the point we move to pre-onboarding

          📋 Edit Candidate Placement Details in SF

          Assignee type
          Workers’ comp code
          Pay rate
          Work location
          Start date
          All other placement fields

          ⚡ Automated — Portal Initiated in SF

          Moved to pre-onboarding initiated

          COI (Conflict of Interest)
          Profile created
          Conditional offer accepted
          Photo upload for badge

          ⚡ Invited to on-site skills assessment

          Automated — candidate invited to schedule

          ⚡ Contractor App invitation launched

          Automated — sent to candidate

          Pre-onboarding steps completed

          Skills tests passed
          Drug screening cleared
          Background check cleared

          Contractor Portal docs

          COI completion
          Profile created
          Conditional offer accepted
          Photo upload for badge

          All items must be completed before talent pool

          AMAT Internal Tracker
          update as required

          ✅ Moved to Talent Pool

          Fully vetted — cleared for assignment

          Uploaded to Fieldglass Workforce Module

          Candidate added to workforce pool in FG

          Ongoing engagement — pooled candidates

          Regular outreach to confirm availability

          Holding — awaiting active job notification from AMAT

          Phase 1 complete — continues to Phase 2

          Phase 2 — Active Job Placement

          Active job placement through to Hired

          Selected candidates assigned to AMAT Active Requisition

          ⚡ Candidates available / interested in shift and start date — automated outreach

          Availability confirmed via automated campaign

          Available candidates — Handoff ready

          Ready for Fieldglass submission

          Candidates submitted

          Profiles submitted in Fieldglass for review

          AMAT reviews — candidates moved to Offer

          Advanced to Offer stage in Fieldglass

          📋 Offer placement updated — Delivery

          Job ID → current job
          Submission ID → new submission
          Placement status moved to Submitted

          ⚡ Phase 2 of onboarding initiated

          Submitted status fires onboarding sequence

          🔄 CS changes workflow to Onboarding Workflow 2

          CS team performs this step after Delivery moves candidate to Offer

          Onboarding steps — second half

          I-9 verification
          Banking info upload
          AMAT document completion
          Raise document completion
          Raise Health & Safety digital training
          ⚡ Fieldglass onboarding steps completed (parallel)

          🎉 Candidates moved to Hired

          Onboarding complete — placement fully active

          Curation job updated

          Candidates set to Assigned to Active Assignment

          👥Who Does What — Quick Reference
          OwnerActionPhase
          Recruiter / CurationScreens candidate, completes pre-handoff steps, edits placement details in SFPhase 1
          SF / AutomatedPortal invite, skills assessment invite, app invitation sentPhase 1
          CandidateCompletes portal (COI, profile, conditional offer, photo)Phase 1
          On-SiteSkills tests, drug screen, BGC, pre-onboarding gate checkPhase 1
          CurationUploads to Fieldglass Workforce Module, ongoing pooled candidate engagementPhase 1
          DeliveryMoves candidate to OFFER — updates Job ID, Submission ID, placement status to SubmittedPhase 2
          CSChanges workflow to Onboarding Workflow 2 after Delivery moves to Offer. Drives I-9, banking, AMAT docs, Raise docs, H&S training to completion.Phase 2
          Curation / CSFG onboarding steps completed, updates curation job to Assigned to Active AssignmentPhase 2
          ✅Full Workflow Checklist

          Phase 1 — Pre-Requisition Vetting

          • Job application form reviewed and on file
          • Phone interview completed
          • Recruiter screening form submitted and reviewed
          • Rehire form completed and cleared (if applicable)
          • HANDOFF — Resume arrives in SF, case received
          • Placement details edited in SF (assignee type, workers’ comp, pay, location)
          • Portal initiated — COI, profile, conditional offer, photo upload sent to candidate
          • Skills tests passed
          • Drug screening cleared
          • Background check cleared
          • Contractor Portal docs complete (COI, profile, conditional offer, photo)
          • AMAT Internal Tracker updated
          • Moved to Talent Pool — fully vetted ✅
          • Uploaded to Fieldglass Workforce Module
          • Ongoing engagement — availability confirmed regularly while holding

          Phase 2 — Active Job Placement

          • Candidate assigned to AMAT Active Requisition
          • Availability confirmed — automated outreach sent
          • Candidate submitted in Fieldglass
          • AMAT reviews — candidate moved to Offer in FG
          • DELIVERY: Job ID → current job, Submission ID → new submission, placement status → Submitted
          • CS: Workflow changed to Onboarding Workflow 2
          • I-9 verification completed
          • Banking info uploaded
          • AMAT documents completed
          • Raise documents completed
          • Raise Health & Safety digital training completed
          • Fieldglass onboarding steps completed
          • Candidate moved to HIRED 🎉 — placement fully active
          • Curation job updated — set to Assigned to Active Assignment
          ✎My Notes
          Onboarding Reference

          Training with Sara — Worker Types & Portal Setup

          Notes from Sara's training session — 04/17/2026. Covers worker type classifications, what AM provides, contact/onboarding flow, and employment verification.

          👤Worker Type Classifications
          TypeWhat It Means
          V2V We are paying a company to pay for a candidate we hired.
          1099 Complicated — not needed at the moment.
          Nana Candidate is incorporated. If they get rejected they need to reapply. The worker is paying for it.
          ONLO We are paying for it.
          IC

          We need to get ID — the following info is required:

          a. Company name
          b. Tax number needed
          c. US is tax exempt
          d. GST / HST required (CA)
          e. Vendor tax ID
          f. Vendor class ID: IC
          📥What AM (Account Manager) Provides
          a. Name / Email
          b. Code (Location) / Postal
          c. Employee type
          d. Start and end date

          A. Assignee Type

          Where you work is what your assignee type is.

          B. Contact

          1. Onboarding Part 1 — Candidate stops at banking.

          2. Onboarding Part 2 — Banking onwards.

          ✅C. Ask AMAT — Employment Verification
          a. Credentials — pending portal documents
          b. Background Check — pending backgrounds
          → Background check reports — upload in placement files
          ✎My Notes
          Click below to add notes
          Onboarding Reference

          📱 What We Collect — Portal Pages

          Visual reference for every screen in the contractor onboarding portal, with explainer notes and employment-type rules.

          RAISE
          Home
          Documents
          MP
          📋Quick Reference — Employment Type by Page
          PageEmployment TypesNotes
          Welcome / Onboarding OverviewAllStep 2 locked until background check clears
          OfferAllConditional offer with merge fields; requires acceptance checkbox
          CredentialsAll (as required)Skipped if no credentials configured; Business Docs section for IC/1099
          Background CheckAllProvider: Checkr. States: Incomplete → Pending
          Assignment DocsAllDocs vary by province, employment type, client config
          Work Eligibility (I-9 & W4)W2 onlyNot shown to IC or 1099 workers
          Banking Info — PlaidW2 CEDefault; "Connect Another Way" leads to manual form
          Banking Info — ManualAllIC/1099 always land here; Plaid not available for IC/1099
          Health & SafetyAllCourses vary by assignment; sync fix via SF "Sync Progress" checkbox
          HomeAllPost-onboarding dashboard; assignments, perks, onboarding status
          DocumentsAllAlways accessible from top nav; signed docs + library
          🏠Page 1 — Welcome to Raise (Onboarding Overview)
          All

          Welcome to Raise!

          We're excited to help you get onboarded for your next opportunity. To ensure a smoother sign up, please have the following ready to start!

          STEP 14/5
          Create an Account
          Profile Information Added
          Personal Information Added
          Offer Signed
          Credentials Uploaded

          What Happens After?

          Once you finish this step, we'll perform a background check and send you an email to invite you to complete Step 2.

          ⏱ Estimated Time: 5–10 Minutes

          STEP 24/6
          Pass Background Check
          Assignment Docs Signed
          Work Eligibility Confirmed
          Banking Information Added
          DEI (Optional)
          Health & Safety Training Completed

          ⏱ Estimated Time: 20–30 Minutes

          Dunder Mifflin

          Regional Manager

          $51/HOUR

          2024-10-01Starts On
          2024-11-01Ends On

          Have questions about your assignment? Our team is here to help!

          Step 2 "Get Started" is locked until the background check in Step 1 is cleared. The button is visible but disabled.
          ✍️Page 2 — Offer
          All
          6/6

          Offer

          Conditional Offer with Dunder Mifflin – Regional Manager

          Congratulations, we are pleased to extend you a conditional offer of employment with Raise.

          Offer Details:

          • Employer: {Paying Company Name}
          • Client: {Client Name}
          • Job Title / Assignment: {Job Title}
          • Pay Rate: ${Pay Rate}
          • Start / End / Pay Cycle: {Dates}

          Conditions:

          1. Health and Safety Training completion
          2. Clear background check results
          3. Required Certifications: {List ordered credentials}

          Questions? Chat, call, or email support@raiserecruiting.com

          I Accept this Conditional Offer
          Assignment Docs
          Signed Agreement
          Banking Info
          Health & Safety

          The contractor must tick "I Accept this Conditional Offer" to enable the Continue button. All merge fields are pre-populated from the assignment record.

          📎Page 3 — Credentials
          All (as required)
          4/6

          Credentials

          Sample Credential (With Exp)

          Candice Pena - Resume.pdf

          Step is skipped entirely if no credentials are configured on the assignment. The "Business Documents" section (Certificate of Incorporation, etc.) only appears for IC/1099 workers.

          🔍Page 4a — Background Check (Incomplete)
          All

          Background Check

          Provider: Checkr

          Screenings: County Criminal, Federal Criminal, Global Watchlist Search.

          Identity Documents Required

          2 documents required — front and back of each. For example:

          🪪 Passport
          🚗 Driver's License

          Background Check Status

          Once submitted, your CS team member will watch for updates.

          Incomplete
          Offer
          Signed Conditional Offer

          ⏳Page 4b — Background Check (Pending)
          All

          Background Check

          Provider: Checkr

          Background Check Status

          Your background check is in progress. Your CS team member will reach out if anything requires action.

          ⏱ Pending
          Offer
          Signed Conditional Offer
          📎 Credentials
          Background Check
          Submit your details
          Clear background check

          After submission, the Identity Documents section disappears and the badge switches to Pending. The contractor can Continue to complete the rest of onboarding while the check processes.

          📝Page 5 — Assignment Docs
          All
          1/5

          Assignment Docs

          Agreements to Sign (Batch 1)

          Please find a list of documents you're required to sign.

          📄 MDA - Application for Employment

          By signing, you confirm all electronic signatures are the legal equivalent of your handwritten signature.

          Agreements to Sign (Batch 2)

          Please find a list of documents you're required to sign.

          📄 2023 TD1 Alberta Provincial Tax Credit Form
          📄 Wandering Worker Policy - CE
          📄 Agreement - Contract Employment
          📄 Ian Martin Group Common Expectations - Code of Conduct
          📄 Employment Standards AB - What You Need to Know
          Assignment Docs
          Signed Agreement
          Banking Info
          Health & Safety

          Documents are grouped into signing batches, each with their own "Continue to Sign" button. Exact documents depend on assignment configuration (province, employment type, client).

          🪪Page 6 — Work Eligibility (I-9 & W4)
          W2 only
          2/6

          Work Eligibility

          Federal Form I-9 & Tax Forms

          The Form I-9 verifies identity and employment authorization. The W4 reports taxable earnings.

          FORM I-9 SECTION 1✓ Completed
          FORM I-9 SECTION 2✓ Completed
          E-VERIFY STATUSPending Result
          TAX FORMS (W4)Not Started

          I'll Do it Later

          Add a Method of Payment

          It may take 3–5 business days to set up your payment details manually.

          Enter the name exactly as it appears on your bank account

          4–17 digits

          Upload a VOID cheque or direct deposit form.

          OR

          Connects automatically. Trusted by 110M+ people.

          All

          Welcome, Mary!

          My Assignments

          Dunder Mifflin

          Regional Manager

          $51/HOUR

          2024-10-01Starts On

          Always accessible from the top nav. Two sections: My Signed Documents (re-downloadable completed e-signatures) and Library Documents (policy/reference docs). Right panel mirrors the Home checklist, with an inline Health & Safety alert card if that step is still pending.
